pages.dataKeywords:
company_size What is your current company size? Use total number of employees across all locations. single_choice data_infrastructure_maturity How would you describe your current data infrastructure? Consider how centralized, standardized, and accessible your key data sources are. ai_experience_level What best describes your organization’s AI experience to date? Include any pilots, proofs of concept, or production deployments. ai_budget_commitment What level of budget is allocated or anticipated for AI initiatives over the next 12 months? An estimate is fine if a formal budget line does not exist yet. leadership_support How strong is executive and leadership support for AI initiatives? Consider sponsorship from C‑suite, business unit heads, and key stakeholders. scale technical_skillset How would you rate your internal technical and data science capabilities? Include data engineering, analytics, ML engineering, and MLOps experience. blog_ai_governance_playbook_2025_11 A 90-Day Playbook for Practical AI Governance 90-day-ai-governance-playbook A concise, action-oriented guide for standing up AI governance in under three months. This 90-day playbook outlines a pragmatic approach to AI governance that balances risk, innovation, and speed. We break the work into three 30-day phases: discovery, design, and rollout. Each phase includes specific deliverables such as a risk register, model inventory, and decision rights matrix.

By the end, you’ll have a lightweight AI governance framework in place that is anchored in real business decisions, not abstract policies. governance_ethics ai governance risk management compliance operating model published blog_ai_governance_board_2026_02 What Boards Need to Know About AI Governance in 2026 board-ai-governance-2026 Key questions directors should ask about AI risk, accountability, and metrics. Boards are being asked to sign off on increasingly ambitious AI roadmaps. Yet, many lack a clear view of AI risks and controls. This article offers a focused checklist of questions for directors: where AI is used, who owns the risk, what safeguards exist, and how performance is monitored.

We also outline how to embed AI governance into existing risk and audit committees rather than creating yet another siloed forum. board risk oversight strategy blog_responsible_genai_policies_2025_07 Designing Responsible GenAI Policies Without Slowing Innovation responsible-genai-policies How to craft practical guardrails for Generative AI that your teams will actually follow. Most GenAI policies fail because they are either too vague or too restrictive. In this piece we propose a middle path built around three tiers of use cases, simple approval workflows, and transparent risk ownership.

We share templates you can adapt, including an acceptable use policy, prompt hygiene checklist, and data classification guide tailored for GenAI. genai policy blog_ai_governance_operating_model_2024_09 Building an AI Governance Operating Model That Sca

pages.dataKeywords: act_rome_city_walking_tour Rome Italy Rome City Walking Tour rome-city-walking-tour walking_tour A relaxed small-group walking tour covering the Trevi Fountain, Spanish Steps, Pantheon, and Piazza Navona with a local guide. act_rome_colosseum_forum_guided Colosseum & Roman Forum Guided Tour colosseum-roman-forum-guided-tour guided_tour Skip-the-line access to the Colosseum, Roman Forum, and Palatine Hill with an expert historian guide and headsets included. act_vatican_museums_sistine Vatican Museums & Sistine Chapel Early Access vatican-museums-sistine-chapel-early-access museum_visit Enter the Vatican Museums before general opening hours and enjoy a guided visit including the Sistine Chapel and St. Peter’s Basilica. act_rome_trastevere_food_tour Trastevere Evening Food Tour trastevere-evening-food-tour food_tour Taste Roman street food, pasta, wines, and gelato in the charming Trastevere neighborhood with a local foodie guide. act_florence_uffizi_highlights Florence Uffizi Gallery Highlights Tour uffizi-gallery-highlights-tour Skip-the-line tour of the Uffizi Gallery focusing on masterpieces by Botticelli, Michelangelo, Leonardo, and Raphael. act_florence_renaissance_walk Florence Renaissance Walking Tour florence-renaissance-walking-tour Discover Florence’s historic center, including the Duomo, Piazza della Signoria, and Ponte Vecchio with a licensed guide. act_venice_grand_canal_boat Venice Grand Canal Boat Tour venice-grand-canal-boat-tour boat_tour Cruise along Venice’s Grand Canal in a small motorboat, passing under iconic bridges and learning the city’s history. act_venice_canal_boat_tour Venice Canal Boat Tour venice-canal-boat-tour A scenic canal boat tour through Venice’s quieter waterways, ideal as an introduction to the city’s layout and stories. act_tokyo_shibuya_night_food Tokyo Japan Shibuya Night Food Tour tokyo-shibuya-night-food-tour Sample yakitori, ramen, and Japanese sweets in Shibuya’s lively backstreets with a bilingual local guide. act_tokyo_sumo_stable_visit Sumo Stable Morning Visit tokyo-sumo-stable-morning-visit other Observe a real sumo training session at a Tokyo stable and learn about sumo traditions and lifestyle. act_kyoto_fushimi_inari_hike Kyoto Fushimi Inari Guided Hike kyoto-fushimi-inari-guided-hike Follow a guide through the famous torii gates of Fushimi Inari Shrine and lesser-known forest trails. act_paris_louvre_skip_line Paris France Louvre Museum Skip-the-Line Tour paris-louvre-museum-skip-the-line See the Mona Lisa and other masterpieces on a guided Louvre visit with fast-track entrance. act_barcelona_gaudi_walking Barcelona Spain Gaudí Modernism Walking Tour barcelona-gaudi-modernism-walking-tour Explore the Eixample district and admire Gaudí’s Casa Batlló, Casa Milà, and Sagrada Família from the outside. act_newyork_met_central_park_combo New York United States Met Museum & Central Park Combo Tour new-york-met-museum-central-park-combo Guided highlights tour of the Metropolitan Museum of Art followe

pages.body: Home Donate Our Work Programs Sponsor a Child Volunteer Events Fundraise Take Action About Contact Donate Write your representative Pick an issue to get a ready-to-customize letter. Advocacy topic Select a topic9 We'll load the right talking points for you. Your ZIP code Find my reps We'll look up your federal and local representatives. Recipients Choose which offices should receive your letter. Advocacy FAQs Share Select an advocacy topic to load a sample letter. Customize your letter We've drafted key points. Use the first paragraph to share why this matters to you. Your story (first paragraph) Aim for at least 20 words so your letter feels personal. Letter preview Your full letter will appear here after you choose a topic. Delivery options Choose how we should deliver your letter. Send email through this site We'll email your letter directly to the offices you select where possible. Generate a print-ready PDF Download and mail or hand-deliver your letter if you prefer. Need help with advocacy? Read the Advocacy FAQs Your information Officials require this to accept your letter. Full name Email Street address Address line 2 (optional) City State Postal code Submit letter By submitting, you authorize us to send this letter on your behalf to the selected officials. pages.body: Home Donate Our Work Programs Sponsor a Child Volunteer Events Fundraise Take Action About Contact Donate Try again Our mission Read full mission Every child deserves a full plate and a fair chance. This page connects you directly to high-impact ways to help provide school meals, emergency food boxes, and nutrition support. Impact at a glance Key ways your support is fighting child hunger. Take action Choose how you want to show up for kids today. Quick actions Donate once Give monthly Sponsor a child Volunteer Run or walk event Start a fundraiser Advocate Urgent volunteer opportunities High-need shifts where extra hands are needed most. View all volunteering Why urgent shifts matter When volunteer spots go unfilled, meal packing lines slow down and fewer children receive food that week. Signing up for an urgent shift helps keep meals moving to kids who need them. Runs and walks for kids Family-friendly events that turn miles into meals. See all events Featured fundraising campaigns Start a personal or team fundraiser in just a few clicks. Loading actions and impact... Ending child hunger through meals, community, and hope. Get Involved Donate Sponsor a Child Volunteer Events Fundraise Advocate Our Work Our Work Programs FAQ About Us Contact Us Legal Privacy Policy Terms & Conditions © 2026 No Child Hungry. All rights reserved.
pages.dataKeywords: increase_school_breakfast_funding Increase School Breakfast Funding Support legislation that expands access to healthy, free or reduced-price school breakfasts for children in low-income communities. child_nutrition_general Protect and Expand Child Nutrition Programs Urge lawmakers to protect and strengthen core child nutrition programs such as SNAP, WIC, and national school meals. summer_meals_programs Strengthen Summer Meals Programs Advocate for better funding and flexibility for summer meal sites so children don’t go hungry when school is out. summer_meals_2025 Summer Meals 2025 summer-meals-2025 Help ensure children have consistent access to nutritious meals all summer long. This peer-to-peer campaign empowers supporters to create personal fundraising pages to keep kids fed when school cafeterias are closed. active peer_to_peer school_breakfast_expansion_2026 School Breakfast Expansion 2026 school-breakfast-expansion-2026 Fuel young minds by expanding universal school breakfast programs in high-need districts. upcoming organizational emergency_food_boxes_2026 Emergency Food Boxes 2026 emergency-food-boxes-2026 Provide rapid-response food boxes to families facing sudden crises, disasters, or displacement. maternal_infant_nutrition_2026 Maternal & Infant Nutrition 2026 maternal-infant-nutrition-2026 Improve nutrition for pregnant women, new mothers, and infants during the first 1,000 days of life. holiday_hunger_match_2025 Holiday Hunger Match 2025 holiday-hunger-match-2025 Every dollar given during the holiday season was doubled by generous matching partners to fight child hunger. archived match back_to_school_lunches_2025 Back-to-School Lunches 2025 back-to-school-lunches-2025 Equip schools with the resources they need to provide nutritious lunches as children return to classrooms. global_child_nutrition_week_2026 Global Child Nutrition Week 2026 global-child-nutrition-week-2026 A focused week of action to fund innovative child nutrition programs across multiple regions. community_pantry_partners_2026 Community Pantry Partners 2026 community-pantry-partners-2026 Support local pantries that provide weekend and evening meals to children and their families. africa_school_meals_drive_2026 Africa School Meals Drive 2026 africa-school-meals-drive-2026 Raise vital funds for large-scale school meal programs across multiple African countries. central_america_nutrition_relief_2026 Central America Nutrition Relief 2026 pages.dataKeywords:
article_pc_patch_221 PC Patch Notes 2.2.1 - March 1, 2026 patch_notes Detailed notes for PC Patch 2.2.1, focusing on stability fixes, minor balance tweaks, and quality-of-life improvements released on March 1, 2026. global stability bug_fixes all_players 2.2.1 article_pc_patch_220 PC Patch Notes 2.2.0 - February 22, 2026 PC Patch 2.2.0 delivers new UI features, matchmaking improvements, and groundwork for upcoming seasonal content. Patch 2.2.0 for PC introduces foundational systems in preparation for the upcoming season. Players will notice updated UI in the lobby, improved match queue estimates, and preliminary balance changes to crowd-control abilities.

Highlights:
- New seasonal UI elements in the home hub.
- Improved matchmaking estimates based on role preferences.
- Initial tuning of crowd-control durations.

These changes pave the way for larger content drops later in March. seasonal_prep 2.2.0 article_patch_215 Update 2.1.5 - Cross-Platform Stability Patch Cross-platform Patch 2.1.5 focuses on crash fixes, performance boosts on all platforms, and networking improvements. cross_platform 2.1.5 article_balance_210 Balance Patch 2.1.0 Notes Balance Patch 2.1.0 delivers wide-ranging hero and weapon adjustments targeting high-level competitive play. balance_patch competitive 2.1.0 article_balance_205 Balance Patch 2.0.5 Notes A follow-up balance patch focused on fine-tuning overperforming builds introduced in 2.0.0. 2.0.5 article_balance_199 Balance Patch 1.9.9 Notes The final balance patch of the 1.x cycle, bringing closing adjustments before the 2.0 overhaul. Balance Patch 1.9.9 wraps up the 1.x series with final tuning passes across the roster. The emphasis is on smoothing out extreme matchups and preparing systems for the upcoming 2.0 update.

Highlights:
- Reduced extreme win-rate outliers in niche compositions.
- Smoothed progression rewards at high ranks.

With 1.9.9, our focus shifts toward long-term systems introduced in 2.0. pre_season 1.9.9 article_tournament_shadow_cup_2025 Shadow Cup 2025 Invitational Tournament esports Announcing the Shadow Cup 2025 Invitational Tournament, featuring the top 16 teams from around the world and a record-breaking prize pool. We are excited to announce the Shadow Cup 2025 Invitational Tournament. Sixteen of the world’s best teams will battle over three weekends in a double-elimination bracket for their share of a $1,000,000 prize pool.

Event Details:
- Dates: August 20–September 6, 2025
- Format: Double-elimination, best-of-five matches
- Broadcast: Official channels with multiple language streams

Stay tuned for group reveals, talent announcements, and ticket information. tournament shadow_cup_2025 tournaments article_tournament_global_championship_2025 Global Championship 2025 Tournament Announcement The Global Championship 2025 Tournament will crown the next world champion with an expanded 24-team format. The Global Championship 2025 Tournament arrives with an expanded 24-team format, introducing

Last updated: — Notifications Turn all notifications on or off Notification Categories Choose which types of in-game news and updates you want to be notified about. Enable only critical alerts (Server Status & Maintenance) Server Status Outages, disruptions, and live service incidents. Enable Server Status notifications Maintenance Scheduled downtime and maintenance windows. Enable Maintenance notifications Patch Notes Balance updates, new features, and bug fixes. Enable Patch Notes notifications Esports News Tournaments, competitions, and esports announcements. Enable Esports News notifications Promotional Offers Limited-time offers, bundles, and in-game promotions. Enable Promotional Offers notifications Community Stories Highlights from the community and featured stories. Enable Community Stories notifications Guides & Tips New player guides, strategy breakdowns, and tips. Enable Guides & Tips notifications Delivery Methods Choose where we send your notifications. You can enable more than one channel. In-site notifications Alerts inside the Rebirth Online news site. Enable in-site notifications Email Messages sent to your linked email address. Enable email notifications Mobile push Push notifications from the Rebirth Online mobile app. Enable mobile push notifications Quiet Hours Set a daily time window when non-critical notifications are muted. Enable quiet hours Enable or disable quiet hours When quiet hours are enabled, most notifications will be held until your quiet window ends. Critical server status and maintenance alerts may still appear. family_caregiver_support Family Caregiver Support family-caregiver-support Advice and emotional support for family members providing care to loved ones. post_surgery_recovery Post-Surgery Recovery post-surgery-recovery Guides for safe and comfortable recovery at home after surgery. q_help_frequency How often does your loved one need help at home? help_frequency single_choice q_main_concern What is your main concern right now? main_concern q_mobility_needs How would you describe your loved one’s mobility? mobility_needs q_living_situation What is your loved one’s current living situation? living_situation overnight_nursing_standard Overnight Nursing Care overnight_care overnight-nursing-care Licensed nurse stays overnight to monitor vitals, manage medications, and provide hands-on support. overnight_companion_care Overnight Companion Care overnight-companion-care Non-medical overnight supervision, companionship, and safety monitoring in the home. memory_dementia_support_basic Memory & Dementia Care (Basic) memory_dementia_care memory-dementia-care-basic Structured routines, cueing, and safety support for individuals in early to mid-stage dementia. memory_dementia_support_intensive Memory & Dementia Care (Intensive) memory-dementia-care-intensive High-touch dementia care with behavior management, wandering prevention, and family coaching. post_surgery_wound_care_visit Post-Surgery Wound Care Visit post_surgery_care post-surgery-wound-care Nurse visit focused on incision assessment, dressing changes, and post-operative education. post_surgery_recovery_visit Post-Surgery Recovery Support post-surgery-recovery-support Comprehensive post-surgical visit including mobility assistance, pain monitoring, and basic wound checks. personal_care_hourly Personal Care personal_care personal-care Hands-on assistance with bathing, dressing, toileting, grooming, and daily personal routines. medication_reminder_checkin Medication Reminders medication_reminders medication-reminders Brief visits or check-ins to prompt, organize, and document medication intake. household_support_light Household Support household_support household-support Light housekeeping, laundry, meal prep, and errands to maintain a safe home environment. phone_care_consult Phone Consultation phone_consultation phone-consultation 30-minute phone call with a care coordinator to review needs and recommend services. assessment_online_intake Online Needs Assessment assessment online-needs-assessment Guided online questionnaire that collects pages.dataKeywords: artwork_conservation_fund Artwork Conservation Fund Supports restoration, preservation, and preventive care for paintings, sculptures, and works on paper in the gallery’s permanent collection. education_outreach_programs Education & Outreach Programs Funds school tours, educator resources, and community workshops that make art accessible to learners of all ages. community_access_fund Community Access Fund Provides free and reduced-price admission for low-income visitors, seniors, and community partner organizations. emerging_artists_residency Emerging Artists Residency Fund Supports residency stipends, studio space, and exhibition opportunities for local emerging artists. modern_collection_highlights Modern Collection Highlights Tour A docent-led overview of key modern works in the collection, ideal for first-time visitors. english paid accessible_morning_highlights Accessible Morning Highlights Tour A slower-paced highlights tour scheduled for weekday mornings, following a wheelchair-accessible route and including frequent seating breaks. family_discovery_tour Family Discovery Tour Interactive, family-friendly tour with hands-on prompts tailored for children ages 6–12 and their caregivers. free architecture_and_public_art_walk Architecture & Public Art Walk Outdoor walking tour exploring the gallery’s architecture and nearby public art installations. donation_suggested evening_contemporary_focus Evening Contemporary Art Focus An in-depth look at current contemporary exhibitions, offered on select weekday evenings. weekend_brunch_tour Weekend Brunch Tour A relaxed weekend tour paired with a light brunch in the gallery café. curators_choice_in_depth Curator’s Choice In-Depth Tour Led by a curator, this tour dives deeply into selected works, focusing on curatorial research and behind-the-scenes stories. lunchtime_express_tour Lunchtime Express Tour A 30-minute snapshot of one featured exhibition, perfect for a lunch break visit. kids_storytime_gallery_walk Kids’ Storytime Gallery Walk A short tour combining picture-book readings with related artworks in the galleries. sensory_friendly_quiet_hours Sensory-Friendly Quiet Hours Tour A low-sensory, small-group tour during quiet hours featuring dimmed lights and reduced ambient noise, suitable for visitors with sensory sensitivities. student_annual_pass Student Annual Pass Discounted membership for currently enrolled students with valid ID, including free general admission and member pricing on tickets. student_youth annual Free general admission, discounted special exhibition tickets, 10% shop discount, and members-only previews. student_plus_annual Student Plus Annual Membership Enhanced student membership with guest passes and access to select member events. All Student Annual benefits plus 2 single-use guest passes and invitations to select member programs. youth_monthly_pass Youth Monthly Pass Flexible month-to-month membership for visitors ages 13–18. monthly Unlimited general admission and discou

pages.body: M orrinsville Gallery Home Visit Exhibitions Tickets Events & Workshops Shop Membership Support My Visit Plan Cart f ig Visit Hours & Location Guided Tours Visitor FAQ Plan your visit Hours Admission Location Accessibility Map & directions Gallery address Get directions in your maps app Guided tours Browse upcoming guided tours and filter by day, time, accessibility, and language. Day Any day Time of day Any time Accessibility Language Any language Start date End date Weekday morning accessible tours Visitor FAQ When should I arrive for my visit? Most visitors spend 60–90 minutes in the galleries. For timed tickets or scheduled tours, arriving 10–15 minutes early allows time for check-in and storing belongings. Is the gallery wheelchair accessible? All public areas of the gallery are step-free or served by lifts. Accessible restrooms are available, and many guided tours follow wheelchair-accessible routes. See the accessibility summary above or contact us with specific needs. Do I need a ticket to enter? General admission policies vary by exhibition. Many displays are free or donation-based, while some special exhibitions and tours are ticketed. Check the Admission and Tickets pages for current details. Stay informed Get occasional updates about new exhibitions, guided tours, and community events. I thought the hallway fight was one of the best choreographed set pieces in modern sci-fi. cmt_0003 lotr_book_fotr_review TolkienFan Totally agree that the pacing in the Shire is slow, but it really pays off once the Fellowship leaves Rivendell. cmt_0004 lotr_movie_fotr_review MovieBuff Your point about the extended edition being the definitive version is spot on. The added character moments make a huge difference. cmt_0005 sci_fi_book_review_mars_2025 I wish you had talked more about the hard-science aspects. pages.dataKeywords: the_fool The Fool the-fool major_arcana none New beginnings, spontaneity, innocence, and a leap of faith into the unknown. Recklessness, naivety, fear of taking a step, or ignoring consequences. A youth steps toward the edge of a cliff with a small pack, white rose, and loyal dog, symbolizing trust, purity, and instinct guiding a new journey. beginnings innocence trust risk potential the_magician The Magician the-magician Manifestation, focused will, resourcefulness, and using skills to create change. Manipulation, scattered energy, unused potential, or trickery. The Magician stands before a table with wand, cup, sword, and pentacle, channeling energy between heaven and earth. manifestation power skill focus resourcefulness the_high_priestess The High Priestess the-high-priestess Intuition, mystery, inner knowing, and the subconscious mind. Secrets, hidden motives, ignoring intuition, or blocked inner voice. A veiled figure sits between black and white pillars, guarding the mysteries behind a curtain decorated with pomegranates. intuition mystery subconscious silence inner_wisdom the_empress The Empress the-empress Abundance, fertility, creativity, nurturing, and sensual pleasure. Creative blocks, dependence, smothering, or neglecting self-care. A crowned figure sits in a lush field with wheat and a flowing river, symbolizing fertility and the flow of life. abundance nurturing creativity fertility comfort the_emperor The Emperor the-emperor Structure, authority, stability, and taking responsibility. Control issues, rigidity, domination, or avoidance of responsibility. A stern ruler on a stone throne with rams carved into it, representing Aries energy, leadership, and discipline. authority structure leadership discipline stability the_lovers The Lovers the-lovers Love, union, aligned values, and meaningful choices from the heart. Disharmony, imbalance, misaligned values, or difficult relationship choices. Two figures stand beneath an angel, with a tree of knowledge and a burning tree representing choice and sacred union. love union choice values relationships strength Strength Inner strength, courage, compassion, and gentle influence over raw instincts. Self-doubt, insecurity, anger, or misusing personal power. A woman calmly closes a lion's jaws, with an infinity symbol above her head, symbolizing spiritual strength and tame courage. courage compassion inner_strength patience resilience the_hermit The Hermit the-hermit Solitude, introspection, spiritual searching, and inner guidance. Isolation, loneliness, withdrawal, or ignoring inner wisdom. An old figure holds a lantern in the dark, illuminating only a few steps ahead, symbolizing the path of inner discovery. introspection solitude wisdom guidance reflection wheel_of_fortune Wheel of Fortune wheel-of-fortune Cycles, turning points, fate, and unexpected shifts in fortune. Resistance to change, bad luck, or feeling stuck in a negative cycle. A rotating wheel with symbols and creatures around it

pages.body: Local Community Guide Home Restaurants & Food Events Volunteering Parks & Outdoors Home Services Childcare Groups & Clubs My Lists Help & How It Works Find step-by-step guidance for searching, filtering, saving lists, joining groups, RSVP'ing, and contacting organizers. Last updated: Not available Search this page Search Results update as you type. This search only looks at this Help page. Jump to a topic Searching & filters Saving to custom lists Groups, events & RSVPs Contacting organizers Troubleshooting Searching and filtering listings Use search and filters on directory and events pages to quickly find places, services, and activities that fit your needs. Search by ZIP code and radius Go to the relevant listing page (for example, Restaurants & Food, Parks & Outdoors, Events, or Volunteering). Enter your ZIP code into the search bar. Adjust the radius control to choose how far from that ZIP code you want to search (for example, 2, 5, 10, or 25 miles). Apply the search. The page shows only Place, Event, or Group results whose ZIP or service area overlaps your chosen radius. If you do not enter a ZIP code, some pages may use a default community area and radius. Filter by price and rating Price filters use fields like average_price_per_person, hourly_rate, or an event's price. Choose a budget range (for example, free, $, $$, $$$). Rating filters use the rating field (0.0–5.0 stars) and review_count. You can ask to see only highly rated options, such as 4.0+ stars. Combine price and rating filters with your ZIP and radius to narrow down the list to your best options. Filter by amenities and suitability Amenities filters are based on specific fields on Place records, for example: Family friendly – uses is_family_friendly (great for restaurants, parks, and childcare providers). Playground – uses has_playground for parks. Dog friendly – uses is_dog_friendly for parks and outdoor areas. Takes reservations – uses takes_reservations for restaurants and cafes. 24/7 emergen

pages.dataKeywords: art_remote_1 Leading Remote Teams: A Practical Playbook for Managers leading-remote-teams-practical-playbook Specific tools and rituals to help managers lead remote teams with clarity, trust, and accountability. Remote leadership requires intentional structure and communication. In this playbook, we break down weekly rhythms, meeting cadences, and feedback loops that keep distributed teams aligned without burning people out... remote_work remote_teams leadership team_productivity Alex Rivera published art_remote_2 5 Rituals of High-Trust Remote Teams 5-rituals-of-high-trust-remote-teams Trust is harder—but not impossible—when your team is remote. These five rituals make it visible and measurable. High-trust remote teams share a set of observable behaviors: transparent decisions, shared planning, consistent follow-up, and space for informal connection. This article outlines five simple rituals you can install over the next 30 days... team_culture Priya Desai art_remote_3 Remote Team Leadership Fundamentals: From One-on-Ones to Outcomes remote-team-leadership-fundamentals A fundamentals guide for managers newly responsible for leading remote teams across time zones. If you are transitioning from in-office to remote leadership, the fundamentals matter more than ever: clear expectations, structured one-on-ones, reliable decision paths, and predictable feedback. This guide walks through each element with templates you can adapt immediately... remote_leadership manager_training Jordan Blake art_remote_4 Designing Asynchronous Collaboration for Global Remote Teams designing-asynchronous-collaboration-for-global-remote-teams Shift your remote team from meeting overload to effective async collaboration. Asynchronous collaboration is the backbone of sustainable global remote teams. By shifting status updates, documentation, and early feedback into async channels, you free your meetings for decisions and problem-solving... async_collaboration Taylor Morgan art_change_1 Change Management 101: Guiding Teams Through Uncertainty change-management-101-guiding-teams A step-by-step framework for managers tasked with leading teams through change. Leading change starts with clarity on why the change matters, who is impacted, and how you will communicate progress. This article introduces a practical four-phase model... change_management communication Maria Chen art_lead_1 Leadership Fundamentals for New Managers leadership-fundamentals-for-new-managers Key mindset shifts and skills every first-time manager needs in the first 90 days. The move from individual contributor to leader is one of the biggest career transitions. We outline the core fundamentals: setting direction, coaching, and giving feedback... art_lead_2 Coaching Conversations That Unlock Performance coaching-conversations-unlock-performance Use these question frameworks to turn status updates into powerful coaching moments. pages.dataKeywords: riverside_park Riverside Park 100 Riverside Park Way Maple Grove Free surface lot off Riverside Park Way; overflow street parking available on weekends. outdoor riverside_community_center Riverside Community Center 120 Riverside Park Way Small free lot behind the center; additional parking along Oak Lane. mixed riverside_playground Riverside Adventure Playground 150 Riverside Park Way Shared with Riverside Park main lot; look for Playground Entrance signs. downtown_childrens_museum Downtown Children's Museum 250 Market Street Suite 200 Downtown Validated parking available in the Market Street Garage; first 2 hours $5 with museum validation. indoor maple_grove_library Maple Grove Public Library 10 Library Plaza Civic Center Underground garage with 3 hours free for library visitors; meter parking on Elm Street. willow_creek_park Willow Creek Park 800 Willow Creek Rd Willow Creek Two small free lots off Willow Creek Rd; street parking fills quickly on weekends. sunset_amphitheater Sunset Amphitheater 50 Sunset View Blvd Sunset Hills Paid event parking lot ($10–$20 depending on event); limited neighborhood street parking. pine_hill_rec_center Pine Hill Recreation Center 400 Pine Hill Ave Pine Hill Large free lot behind the gym; accessible spaces near main entrance. lakeside_botanical_garden Lakeside Botanical Garden 200 Lakeview Dr Lakeside Paid lot ($5 flat rate); free bike parking near the main gate. valley_view_mall Valley View Mall 1000 Valley View Dr Valley View Multi-level free parking garages A–C; family parking spaces near entrances. oakridge_elementary_school Oakridge Elementary School 75 Oakridge Ln Oakridge School lot open for public events only; follow event signage. little_oaks_play_cafe Little Oaks Play Café 45 Maple Ave Suite 3 Old Maple Metered street parking on Maple Ave; free 2-hour parking on nearby side streets. bright_steps_gymnastics Bright Steps Gymnastics 600 Commerce Way Unit B Commerce Park Shared free lot in front of the building; additional parking on the south side. adventure_trampoline_park Adventure Trampoline Park 850 Skyway Blvd Skyway District Large free parking lot surrounding the building. meadowbrook_farm Meadowbrook Family Farm 300 Meadowbrook Rd Meadowbrook Free gravel lot near farm entrance; follow signs for event parking. winterfair_grounds Winterfair Grounds at Lakeside 500 Festival Way Event parking lots P1 and P2 ($10 cash or card); rideshare drop-off at Gate 2. historic_main_street Historic Main Street 1 Main Street Old Town Several paid public lots along Main Street; limited free street parking on side streets. skyline_plaza Skyline Plaza 700 Skyline Terrace Skyline Terrace Underground paid parking; validation available at select restaurants and venues. family_fun_basic Family Fun Basic Membership Perfect for families who attend a few events each season. positioning where power is not available or not preferred. lever_hoists Lever Hoists Ratcheting lever hoists for tensioning, pulling, and lifting in tight or difficult-to-access areas. beam_trolleys pages.dataKeywords: aq_primary_concerns What are your primary smile concerns? Select all that apply. multiple_choice concerns aq_teeth_areas Which teeth bother you the most? This helps us focus recommendations on specific areas. aq_result_timeline How quickly are you hoping to see results? For example, before an upcoming event or within a certain number of months. single_choice goals aq_budget_range What is your approximate budget for improving your smile? Move the slider to the maximum amount you’re comfortable investing. slider constraints aq_treatment_duration_preference How long are you comfortable being in treatment? Shorter treatments may limit which options are suitable. aq_discreet_importance How important is it that your treatment is discreet or nearly invisible? For example, clear aligners vs. more noticeable options. aq_prior_treatment Have you had orthodontic or cosmetic dental treatment before? Include braces, clear aligners, veneers, or bonding. medical_history aq_sensitivity Do you currently experience tooth or gum sensitivity? This can affect which whitening and cosmetic options are recommended. aq_whitening_vs_straightening_priority What is your top priority for treatment? Choose the goal that matters most right now. aq_appointment_frequency How often can you visit the clinic for appointments? Some treatments require more frequent visits than others. aq_visit_style Do you prefer fewer, longer visits or more, shorter visits? We’ll use this to match you with treatments that fit your schedule. aq_upcoming_event Do you have an upcoming event you’re planning your new smile around? For example, a wedding, graduation, or important trip. text aq_health_conditions Do you have any medical conditions or take medications that affect your oral health? Examples include diabetes, osteoporosis, or blood thinners. aq_additional_notes Is there anything else we should know about your smile or goals? Share any details that haven’t been covered yet. other whitening Teeth Whitening teeth_whitening In-office and take-home cosmetic teeth whitening treatments to brighten stained or discolored teeth. veneers Veneers Porcelain and composite veneer options to reshape, resize, and improve the appearance of front teeth. invisalign Invisalign & Clear Aligners Clear aligner treatments such as Invisalign to straighten teeth discreetly. other_cosmetic Other Cosmetic Treatments Additional cosmetic services such as bonding, contouring, and smile makeovers. ao_primary_crooked_teeth Crooked teeth crooked_teeth ao_primary_stained_teeth Stained or yellow teeth stained_teeth ao_primary_chipped_worn Chipped or worn edges chipped_worn_edges ao_primary_gaps Gaps between teeth gaps_between_teeth ao_primary_short_teeth Short or worn-down teeth short_worn_teeth ao_primary_uneven_gums Uneven gumline uneven_gumline ao_primary_old_dental_work Old crowns, fillings, or bonding I don’t like old_dental_work ao_areas_front_top Front top teeth front_top_teeth ao_areas_front_bottom Front bottom teeth fron

pages.dataKeywords: feeder_cattle Feeder cattle Weaned calves and yearlings intended for backgrounding or feedlot finishing, including feeder steers and heifers. bulls Bulls Seedstock and commercial bulls offered for breeding, including Angus, Hereford, and composite bulls. cow_calf_pairs Cow-calf pairs Cows sold with calves at side, including young pairs and 3-in-1 packages. bred_cows Bred cows Confirmed bred cows without calves at side, including spring- and fall-calving groups. bred_heifers Bred heifers Heifers confirmed bred with known calving windows, often synchronized and AI or natural-service bred. stocker_calves Stocker calves Lightweight calves suitable for grazing or backgrounding before entering a feedlot. other Other Specialty or mixed lots that do not fit standard categories, such as cull cows or mixed herd dispersals. user_seller_1 Sam Seller High Plains Cattle Co. seller@example.com Kansas Dodge City seller email_and_in_site user_buyer_1 Jordan Cattlebuyer Circle J Ranch jordan@example.com Nebraska Broken Bow buyer in_site_messages_only user_seller_2 Maria Stockman Red River Livestock maria@redriverlivestock.com Texas Wichita Falls buyer_and_seller email_only interest_user_buyer_1_feeder_cattle interest_user_buyer_1_bred_heifers interest_user_buyer_1_stocker_calves interest_user_buyer_1_bred_cows interest_user_buyer_1_cow_calf_pairs interest_user_buyer_1_bulls lot_15_hereford_bred_cows_ks 15 Hereford bred cows - Kansas Group of 15 Hereford bred cows, mostly 3–5 years old, confirmed bred to a low birthweight Angus bull. Gentle disposition, home-raised, solid-mouth cattle. Vaccinated this fall and poured. Selling in one group from Dodge City, KS. Hereford online_timed scheduled per_head bred cows spring calving lot_draft_red_angus_bred_heifers_ne 16 Red Angus bred heifers - Nebraska (draft) Nice set of 16 Red Angus bred heifers, AI bred to calving-ease Red Angus bulls. Good disposition, pelvic measured and vaccinated. Listing currently in draft status pending final photos and video. Red Angus heifer draft bred heifers draft listing lot_ok_angus_heifers_1 8 Angus bred heifers - Stillwater, OK Eight head of black Angus bred heifers, synchronized and AI bred to calving-ease Angus bulls. Due to calve in April/May. Gentle, cake-broke, and easy to handle. Located near Stillwater, Oklahoma. Angus Oklahoma Stillwater live Angus heifer AI bred lot_ok_angus_heifers_2 12 Angus bred heifers - Enid, OK Twelve home-raised black Angus bred heifers, exposed to low birthweight Angus bulls. Mostly March/April calvers. Sound and easy-fleshing set ready to go to grass. Enid Enid Cattle Co. lot_ok_angus_heifers_3 10 Angus heifers - Tulsa, OK Ten head of black Angus heifers confirmed bred 60–90 days, mostly moderate-framed and easy-fleshing. Good feet and udders on dams. Located just outside Tulsa, OK. pages.body: Livestock Auctions Home All Auctions Feeder Cattle Bulls Cow-Calf Pairs Sell Livestock My Listings Watchlist Saved Searches Profile AA Profile Messages My Listings Watchlist Saved Searches Saved Searches Review, rename, or delete your saved auction searches, and control in-site alerts for each one. Loading saved searches… We could not load your saved searches. Please check your connection and try again. Retry About saved searches Saved searches let you quickly re-run your favorite criteria across all livestock auctions and optionally receive in-site alerts when new matching lots are posted. Tips Use clear names like 'TX bred heifers < $2,000' so you can recognize searches at a glance. Turn alerts on only for the searches you actively monitor. You can always create new saved searches from the All Auctions page after applying filters. Rename saved search Update the name used to identify this saved search. Search name Cancel Save Delete this saved search? This will remove the saved search and its alert settings. This action cannot be undone. pages.dataKeywords: sg_jmls journal_jmls Journal of Machine Learning Systems This journal charges an article processing charge of approximately USD 1,800 for standard research articles, with waivers and discounts available for authors from low- and middle-income countries. Initial editorial decisions are typically issued within 6–8 weeks of submission, with full peer review normally completed within 45 days. research_article review_article short_communication methods_article perspective cc_by cc_by_sa sg_ijcsa journal_ijcsa International Journal of Computer Science Advances The standard APC for IJCSA is USD 1,400 per article, with partial waivers considered on a case-by-case basis for authors lacking institutional support. Most manuscripts receive an initial decision within approximately 10–12 weeks, although complex reviews may require additional time. editorial cc_by_nc sg_ojci journal_ojci Open Journal of Computational Intelligence OJCI levies an APC of approximately USD 1,200 per accepted article, with generous waivers for authors from low-income regions and for high-quality open datasets or software contributions. The editorial office aims to provide an initial peer review decision within 4–6 weeks and final acceptance, where applicable, within 60 days of submission. sg_jes journal_jes Journal of Environmental Studies The Journal of Environmental Studies charges an APC of USD 1,100 for research articles, with lower rates for short communications and no fees for invited editorials. Most manuscripts receive an initial decision within 6–9 weeks; full review and revision cycles typically conclude within 90 days. sg_eci journal_eci Environmental Climate Impacts The standard APC is USD 1,350 for full-length research and review articles, with reduced fees for short communications and extensive waiver options. Initial peer review decisions are typically made within 7 weeks of submission, with many manuscripts accepted within 3 months. sg_cto journal_cto Cardiology Trials & Outcomes Cardiology Trials & Outcomes charges an APC of USD 1,600 for full-length clinical trial reports, with lower fees for short communications and case reports. The median time to first decision is approximately 7 weeks, with accepted clinical trials typically published online within 4–5 months. clinical_trial case_report sg_nbr journal_nbr Neurology & Brain Research Neurology & Brain Research charges a typical APC of USD 1,450 for research and clinical trial articles, with lower charges for brief reports. The journal targets an initial decision within 6 weeks and final acceptance within 10–12 weeks for most manuscripts. sg_gphi journal_gphi Global Public Health Insights APCs range from USD 300 to 1,200 depending on the corresponding author's country income level, with automatic waivers for many low-income settings. Most manuscripts receive an initial decision within 8 weeks of submission. sg_uah journal_uah Urban Air & Health Urban Air & Health charges an APC of USD 1,000 for full articles and U

pages.body: Menu Begin Boating.com Home Safety Basics How-To Guides Checklists Tools Courses Glossary My Learning Path Boat Capacity Calculator Enter your boat details to estimate a safe maximum passenger count and save it into a trip plan. Boat details Boat length (ft) Boat width / beam (ft) Boat type Select boat type Open motorboat Pontoon Sailboat Personal watercraft (PWC) Other Water type Select water type Inland lake or river Coastal / nearshore Offshore ocean Other Calculate capacity Always follow the capacity plate on your boat if present; this tool provides a conservative estimate, not a legal limit. Recommended capacity Enter your boat details above and select Calculate capacity to see a recommended maximum passenger count. Max 0 passengers How this is calculated: Important notes: Add to trip plan Dismiss Save to trip plan Name this configuration so you can reference it when planning a specific outing. Trip name Planned passenger count (optional) Use this to check that your planned group stays within the recommended maximum. Save trip plan Cancel About this tool This calculator uses common small-boat guidelines and your selected water type to suggest a conservative passenger capacity. It is intended as a planning aid for new boaters. Always obey your boat's official capacity plate and local regulations. Reduce passenger count in poor weather or rough water. Distribute weight evenly and keep heavy gear low in the boat. Capacity safety tips Count people, not just seats. Children and adults still count as full passengers. Account for fuel, batteries, coolers, and gear when estimating total weight. Stay well under the maximum in unfamiliar waters or at night. Ask guests to stay seated when underway to avoid sudden shifts in weight. Begin Boating Boating how-to and safety blog About Contact Safety Basics Checklists Tools Courses © 2024 Begin Boating. All rights reserved.

pages.body: Menu Begin Boating.com Home Safety Basics How-To Guides Checklists Tools Courses Glossary My Learning Path Engine Maintenance Scheduler Configure your engine details and generate a maintenance schedule. Configure your engine schedule Engine type* Select engine type Two-stroke outboard Four-stroke outboard Inboard Sterndrive Other / not listed Use the type shown on your engine label or manual. Usage frequency* How often is this engine used? Used daily Used weekly Used monthly Used only in-season Choose the option that matches your typical season. Water type* Where do you primarily run this engine? Freshwater Saltwater Mixed fresh and saltwater Schedules can be more aggressive in saltwater. Schedule start date* Use the date you want this maintenance plan to begin. Reminder frequency* How often should reminders appear? Every month Every 3 months Every 6 months Every 12 months You can always adjust this and regenerate later. All fields are required to build your schedule. You can share or bookmark this setup using the URL in the sidebar. Generate schedule Group tasks by: Calendar month Interval Schedule preview Tasks will appear here after you generate a schedule. Enter your engine details above and select Generate schedule to see your maintenance plan. SAVE SCHEDULE After you are happy with the schedule preview, add an optional note and save it to your tools. Notes (optional) Save schedule Share or restore this setup Copy this link to reopen the scheduler with the same engine details. The form and preview will match these settings whenever the link is used. Begin Boating Boating how-to and safety blog About Contact Safety Basics Checklists Tools Courses © 2024 Begin Boating. pages.dataKeywords: art_safety_beginner_lifejackets Beginner's Guide to Life Jackets and PFDs beginners-guide-life-jackets-pfds safety_basics other Learn how to choose, fit, and maintain a life jacket so every passenger on board stays safe. This beginner-focused guide explains the different types of life jackets (PFDs), how to read the labels, and how to choose the right size for adults and children. It also covers inspection checklists and when to replace your gear. beginner Beginner Safety Gear Life Jackets all_sizes art_safety_beginner_right_of_way 7 Essential Right-of-Way Rules for New Boaters essential-right-of-way-rules-new-boaters Understand who goes first on the water so you can avoid close calls and collisions. This article breaks down seven core right-of-way rules using simple language and diagrams. You’ll learn how to approach other boats, what navigation lights mean, and how to pass or overtake safely. Navigation Rules Safety Basics art_safety_beginner_docking Docking Without Drama: Basic Dock Maneuvers for Beginners docking-without-drama-basic-maneuvers Step-by-step docking tips that help new boaters avoid bumps, scrapes, and panic at the marina. Learn how to plan your approach, use wind and current to your advantage, and communicate with crew while docking. Includes a simple pre-docking checklist you can follow every time. Docking Boat Handling up_to_26_ft art_safety_beginner_fuel Fueling Your Boat Safely: A Quick Guide for First-Time Owners fueling-your-boat-safely-beginners Avoid spills, fires, and fumes with these simple fueling steps every beginner should know. This guide covers where to position passengers, how to ground the nozzle, and what to do before and after you fuel. It also explains common fueling mistakes and how to prevent them. Fuel Safety Gas Dock art_safety_cold_water Cold Water Survival Basics for Small Boat Operators cold-water-survival-basics-small-boats Understand the risks of cold water immersion and what to do if someone falls overboard. This article explains the stages of cold water shock, how to use the Heat Escape Lessening Position (HELP), and what gear you should always have ready when boating in cold conditions. intermediate Cold Water Man Overboard Safety art_safety_thunderstorm_under_26_2022 Thunderstorm Emergency Actions for Boats Under 26 ft thunderstorm-emergency-actions-boats-under-26ft Step-by-step actions to take before, during, and after a thunderstorm in a small boat. Learn how to spot building thunderstorms, when to head for shelter, and what to do if you are caught on open water in a boat under 26 ft. Includes radio call templates and a quick storm checklist. all_levels Thunderstorm Emergency Weather Small Boats art_safety_thunderstorm_under_26_2025 Updated Thunderstorm Emergency Checklist for Small Powerboats (Under 26 ft) updated-thunderstorm-emergency-checklist-small-powerboats-under-26ft The latest, field-tested thunderstorm emergency checklist tailored to open boats under 26 ft. pages.body: Skip to main content eco Conservation Hub Home Learn Events Action Planner Carbon Footprint Calculator Lifestyle Quiz Donate Volunteer Dashboard Loading your latest carbon assessment... Carbon Footprint Calculator Enter a few details about your household to estimate your annual carbon emissions. Household size* Number of people who usually live in your home. Bedrooms Optional. Helps approximate home size. Weekly driving miles Approximate total miles driven by your household each week. Vehicle fuel type Select fuel type Gasoline Diesel Hybrid Electric Other / mixed Monthly electricity (kWh) Use a recent bill if available. Primary heating type Select heating type Natural gas Electric Oil Other None Monthly natural gas (therms) Only if you use natural gas for heating, water, or cooking. Calculate footprint Your baseline annual footprint Run a calculation above to see your household's estimated emissions. Set a reduction goal Define a named goal based on your most recent assessment. Carbon reduction goal details Calculate your footprint above to enable goal setting. Goal name* For example, 'Cut car emissions by 25%' or '2024 household target'. Reduction target (%)* How much would you like to reduce your annual emissions from this baseline? Target emissions preview Based on your current baseline assessment. – Save goal Active carbon reduction goal No active goal is linked to your latest assessment yet. View this goal in My Dashboard eco Conservation Hub Evidence-based resources and tools to support environmental conservation. pages.dataKeywords: act_1 Switch to LED Bulbs Replace incandescent bulbs with ENERGY STAR-certified LED bulbs. Swap out older incandescent or CFL bulbs for high-efficiency LED bulbs in your most-used fixtures (kitchen, living room, exterior lights). Focus first on the 5–10 fixtures you use most. LEDs use up to 80% less energy and last much longer. Energy home_energy lighting low_cost beginner act_2 Lower Thermostat by 2°F Reduce heating or cooling setpoint by two degrees. Adjust your thermostat two degrees lower in winter (or higher in summer) and stick with it for at least a month. Most households barely notice the difference in comfort, but energy savings can add up significantly over a year. heating_cooling behavior_change act_3 Weekly Car-Free Day Commit to one day per week without driving. Choose one day each week where you avoid using a personal car altogether. Use walking, cycling, public transit, or carpooling instead. Start with local errands and commutes that are easiest to shift. Transportation car_free commuting climate act_4 Home Energy Audit (DIY) Conduct a basic do-it-yourself home energy audit. Use an online checklist or your utility’s guide to inspect drafts around windows and doors, check insulation, identify phantom power loads, and note old appliances. Create a list of top three fixes to address over the next three months. audit planning act_5 Smart Power Strips Install smart or switched power strips on electronics clusters. Identify areas with many electronics (TV, gaming console, computer setup) and plug them into smart or manually switched power strips. Turn them off fully when not in use to cut standby power draw. phantom_loads electronics act_6 Meatless Mondays Go vegetarian at least one day a week. Plan and prepare plant-based meals every Monday (or another chosen day). Focus on simple substitutions like beans, lentils, and tofu in place of beef or lamb, which have the highest carbon footprints. Food diet low_carbon household act_7 Install Low-Flow Showerheads Upgrade showerheads to WaterSense-certified models. Replace at least one high-use showerhead with a low-flow model that meets WaterSense standards. This can cut water use and the energy needed to heat that water, often without noticeable change in comfort. Water water_conservation bathroom home_improvement act_8 Compost Kitchen Scraps Start a small compost bin for food scraps. Set up a countertop or under-sink compost pail for fruit and vegetable scraps, coffee grounds, and yard waste. Use a backyard pile, tumbler, or municipal green bin if available to divert organics from landfill. Waste waste_reduction food_waste soil_health act_9 Eliminate Single-Use Bottles Carry a reusable bottle and avoid buying bottled water. Invest in a durable reusable water bottle and keep it with you when commuting, at work, or during errands. Refill from tap or filtered water instead of purchasing single-use plastic bottles. Plastic plastic_pollution zero_waste act_10 Upgrade to Efficient Appliances Plan

pages.dataKeywords: weave_poles Weave Poles Focused practice on weave pole entries, footwork, and speed for competition agility. jumping Jumping Bar jumps, panel jumps, and spread jumps used in agility courses. relay_teams Relay Teams Team-based relay agility runs with baton-style handoffs between dogs. tunnel_runs Tunnel Runs Straight and curved tunnel sequences for building confidence and speed. contact_zones Contact Zones Training on A-frame, dogwalk, and teeter contacts with reliable stop positions. rally_obedience Rally Obedience Patterned heelwork and obedience exercises performed on rally courses. trick_training Trick Training Fun tricks and freestyle moves used for demos and confidence building. scent_work Scent Work Nosework games and formal scent detection exercises. flyball Flyball Relay racing over jumps with a spring-loaded ball box. dock_diving Dock Diving Distance and height jumping from a dock into water. herding_instinct Herding Instinct Introductory herding work on sheep, ducks, or cattle. urban_walks Urban Walks Leash manners and confidence in busy city environments. therapy_visits Therapy Visits Volunteer visits to schools, hospitals, and senior centers. puppy_foundation Puppy Foundation Early life skills including recalls, focus, and body awareness. off_leash_hiking Off-Leash Hiking Trail adventures with focus on recall and trail manners. foil_title Foil Title Adds a metallic foil effect to your ad’s main title line. extra_icons Extra Icons Includes additional paw print and ribbon icons in your layout. photo_retouch Photo pages.body: SC Santa's Castle Holiday Attraction Home Tickets & Pricing Schedule & Activities My Night Groups & Parties Getting Here Accessibility Contact Us Cart Contact Us Use this form to send a question or request about your visit. Our team typically responds within one to two business days. Subject / Topic* Loading topics... Choose the area your question is about so we can direct it to the right team. Your name* Email address* We will only use this email to reply to your message. Your message* Send message ← Back to Home While you wait for a reply During our Christmas season, messages are checked daily by the Santa's Castle team. If your question is about a specific visit, please include your planned date, arrival time, and any ticket or booking details in your message so we can help more quickly. For accessibility or travel questions, mentioning your preferred entrance, mobility needs, or transit route (for example, a bus number) is especially helpful. SC Santa's Castle A local Christmas attraction filled with lights, music, and holiday stories. pages.dataKeywords: act_santa_cabin_181630 Santa's Cozy Cabin Meet & Greet A relaxed meet & greet with Santa in his cozy cabin, perfect for family photos and wish lists. santa_experience families Santa's Cabin – North Village indoor Wheelchair-accessible ramp and low photo bench available; flash can be reduced on request. act_santa_storytime_181900 Santa Storytime by the Fire Join Santa for a cozy storytime featuring classic Christmas tales by the indoor lodge fireplace. Lodge Fireplace Corner Seating reserved for wheelchair users and low-sensory seating available near exits. act_light_northern_181700 Northern Lights Synchronized Show A choreographed light show set to orchestral holiday music in the main plaza. light_show Main Plaza – Light Tree outdoor Accessible viewing area with level ground and limited strobe effects. act_light_finale_181930 Winter Aurora Finale Light Show End your evening with our grand finale of sweeping lights and festive music. all_ages Riverfront Spectator Lawn Wheelchair risers located at the back of the lawn; assistive listening devices available on request. act_workshop_ornaments_181615 Elf Workshop: Ornament Decorating Create your own keepsake ornament with help from Santa's crafty elves. All supplies included. workshop_craft Elf Workshop Barn – Table A Tables at wheelchair-friendly height; fragrance-free glues available. act_workshop_snowflakes_181800 Family Snowflake Craft Lab Cut, paint, and glitter your own paper snowflakes in this family-friendly craft lab. Creative Corner – Indoor Pavilion Quiet corner available for guests with sensory needs; reach tools provided. act_music_carolers_182000 Carolers on the Green Traditional carols performed live by our costumed choir under the sparkling trees. live_music Village Green Stage Paved paths and seating area available; moderate amplification used. act_parade_arrival_101730 Santa's Grand Arrival Parade Watch Santa arrive with his elves, toy soldiers, and marching band in this lively mini-parade. show Main Plaza Parade Route Accessible curbside viewing along the North Gate side of the route. act_workshop_gingerbread_171500 Gingerbread House Decorating Workshop Build and decorate your own gingerbread house with icing, candy, and expert tips. Baker's Hall – Kitchen Studio Gluten-free gingerbread kits available by request; seating provided. act_music_jazz_171900 Holiday Jazz Trio at the Lodge Smooth seasonal jazz standards performed live in the warmth of the lodge. adults Evergreen Lodge – Hearth Room Table service available; low lighting, but pathways kept clear. act_train_polar_181600 Polar Express Mini Train Ride A short, family-friendly train ride around the illuminated village loop. other Mini Train Depot – Central Loop mixed One accessible carriage available; boarding ramp at depot. act_light_tunnel_191830 Twinkle Tunnel Walkthrough Stroll through a tunnel of thousands of synchronized twinkling lights. Twinkle Tunnel – Riverside Path Smooth, paved path; mild pulsing lights, no rapid str

pages.dataKeywords: art_advanced_anchors_overhung_2024 Redundant Anchor Systems for Overhung Indoor Walls redundant-anchor-systems-overhung-indoor-walls Design and inspection strategies for redundant, multidirectional anchors on steep lead walls in modern gyms. This article covers advanced principles for building and inspecting redundant anchor systems on overhung terrain. Topics include bolt spacing on arêtes, direction-of-load analysis, master point equalization techniques, backup strategies for fixed-top-rope anchors, and failure case studies from indoor environments... Dana Keller advanced anchor building redundancy lead climbing gym operations safety art_load_testing_anchors_2023 Load Testing Modern Indoor Anchors load-testing-modern-indoor-anchors Empirical data on shear and outward force performance for common indoor anchor configurations. Indoor anchor design has evolved alongside wall construction techniques. In this study we instrumented common anchor layouts and recorded forces during leader falls, top-rope loads, and dynamic lowers. We compare stainless glue-ins, mechanical bolts, and resin-bonded hardware, and provide recommendations for factor-of-safety and inspection intervals... Miguel Alvarez load testing risk management art_anchor_cleaning_transitions_2025 Efficient Anchor Cleaning and Transition Techniques for Lead Instructors efficient-anchor-cleaning-transition-techniques Standardized methods for teaching safe, efficient anchor cleaning in crowded indoor settings. Lead instructors must balance safety, clarity, and throughput when teaching anchor cleaning in busy gyms. This article outlines step-by-step transition flows for lower-through and re-thread scenarios, discusses common error patterns in new leaders, and recommends communication scripts and backup systems suitable for group instruction... Priya Natarajan instruction teaching methods lead_climbing art_anchor_fundamentals_2022 Indoor Anchor Building Fundamentals indoor-anchor-building-fundamentals A step-by-step guide to basic indoor anchor construction for new staff and instructors. This foundational guide introduces SERENE principles, common indoor hardware, and simple redundant anchor configurations for top-rope setups... Jordan Smith intermediate top rope training art_dynamic_belay_2024 Dynamic Belay Techniques for Indoor Lead Falls dynamic-belay-techniques-indoor-lead-falls How to coach modern dynamic belay technique without compromising fall clearance. Dynamic belaying is now standard practice in high-end lead gyms. We break down stance, rope handling, and decision-making for advanced belayers, with drills for instructors... Casey Nguyen belaying instructor training art_youth_bouldering_curriculum_2023 Designing a Youth Bouldering Curriculum designing-a-youth-bouldering-curriculum Age-appropriate progression ideas for youth bouldering programs. Youth bouldering programs require thoughtful progression to develop movement literacy while minimizing overuse injuries... Elena Park youth p

We do not store your IBANs. Use this as a read-only helper when sending or receiving payments. IBAN to validate Validate Spaces are ignored automatically. Dubai IBANs usually start with AE. ← Back to IBAN tools Validating IBAN… About this validator Checks IBAN length, country format, and checksum where applicable. Attempts to match the bank using Dubai bank code data. Shows a structured breakdown to help you compare with bank and branch details. Quick tips Always copy IBANs from official bank sources or statements. Compare the bank name shown here with your payee's bank. If the bank is unknown, double-check the IBAN before sending funds. pages.body: Hudson Counseling Home Book an Appointment Find a Therapist Group Programs Self-Assessments Fees & Insurance Crisis Support Reading List Self-Assessments About these self-assessments These brief questionnaires are designed to help you reflect on your mood, anxiety, and related experiences. They can be a useful starting point for a conversation about your mental health. Results are informational only and are not a diagnosis or a replacement for an evaluation with a licensed mental health professional. If you are in immediate distress or thinking about harming yourself or others, please seek in-person help or call emergency services right away rather than using an online self-assessment. pages.body: Hudson Counseling Home Book an Appointment Find a Therapist Group Programs Self-Assessments Fees & Insurance Crisis Support Reading List Contact We'd love to hear from you Use this form to send questions about services, scheduling, insurance, or billing. We'll get back to you as soon as we can during business hours. If you're in crisis or need immediate help This form is not monitored 24/7 and should not be used for emergencies. We typically review messages during regular office hours. If you are in immediate danger or thinking of harming yourself or someone else, please call 911 (or your local emergency number) right away. You can also call or text 988 in the U.S. to reach the Suicide & Crisis Lifeline, available 24/7. You are still welcome to use this form for non-urgent questions related to crisis support or safety planning. pages.dataKeywords: individual_50min individual_counseling Individual Counseling (50 minutes) Standard individual therapy session focused on a wide range of concerns such as anxiety, depression, and stress. in_person online individual_80min_intake Individual Counseling Intake (80 minutes) Extended first session to review history, goals, and create an initial treatment plan. couples_75min couples_counseling Couples Counseling (75 minutes) Relationship-focused counseling for partners seeking to improve communication and connection. family_therapy_75min family_therapy Family Therapy (75 minutes) Therapy session for families working on communication, boundaries, and conflict. group_session_90min group_session Group Therapy Session (90 minutes) Facilitated group therapy for topics such as social anxiety, depression support, and trauma recovery. free_consult_15min free_consult_15 Free 15-Minute Consultation Brief telehealth consultation to discuss fit, availability, and answer questions before starting therapy. phq9 phq_9 Depression Self-Assessment (PHQ-9) A brief, evidence-based questionnaire to help screen for symptoms of depression over the last two weeks. This self-assessment is for educational purposes only and does not provide a diagnosis. If you are concerned about your results, please contact a licensed mental health professional. gad7 gad_7 Anxiety Self-Assessment (GAD-7) A short screening tool to assess the severity of generalized anxiety symptoms. Results from this tool are not a substitute for a professional evaluation. If anxiety is impacting your daily life, consider speaking with a therapist or healthcare provider. pts_screen Post-Traumatic Stress Screener A brief questionnaire to help identify common post-traumatic stress symptoms following difficult or traumatic experiences. This screener cannot confirm or rule out PTSD. For a full assessment and support, please consult with a qualified mental health clinician. wellbeing_checkin other General Wellbeing Check-In A short check-in covering mood, stress, sleep, and support to help you reflect on your current wellbeing. This check-in is informational only and does not replace a mental health evaluation or medical advice. sf_county_crisis_line San Francisco County Crisis Line 415-555-0100 local San Francisco County, CA all_ages 24/7 For anyone in San Francisco County experiencing an emotional or mental health crisis. sf_youth_crisis_line San Francisco Youth Crisis Line 415-555-0120 San Francisco, CA youth Confidential support for teens and young adults in crisis. sf_warm_line San Francisco Peer Warm Line 415-555-0135 San Francisco Bay Area, CA adults 7 days a week, 7:00 am–11:00 pm Peer support line for non-emergency emotional support. bay_area_domestic_violence Bay Area Domestic Violence Hotline 510-555-0180 Support, safety planning, and shelter referrals for people experiencing relationship violence. national_suicide_lifeline National Suicide & Crisis Lifeline national United States Call or text 988, or chat

pages.dataKeywords:
ux-telehealth-onboarding-2024 Designing Frictionless Onboarding for a Telehealth App telehealth-app-onboarding-ux-case-study How we reduced patient drop-off by 37% in the first session of a telehealth platform. Maya Ortiz user_experience case_study healthcare ux_case_study ux-fintech-saving-habits-2023 Nudging Better Saving Habits in a Fintech App fintech-saving-habits-ux-case-study Behavioral design techniques that increased weekly savings actions by 24%. This UX case study explores how we used behavioral science to encourage consistent saving in a consumer fintech app.

We introduced goal-based saving jars, progress visualizations, and just-in-time prompts based on payday predictions. The design emphasized clarity and reduced cognitive load by limiting choices at each step.

Post-launch analytics showed a 24% increase in weekly savings actions and a 15% reduction in users withdrawing from their savings jars prematurely. Jared Lin fintech ux-education-learning-paths-2024 Personalized Learning Paths for an EdTech Platform personalized-learning-paths-ux Reimagining course discovery and progress for 120K+ active students. For a fast-growing EdTech platform, we redesigned the course discovery and learning path experience.

Through diary studies and analytics, we learned that learners struggled to understand which course to take next and how close they were to their goals. We introduced guided paths, clear skill outcomes, and a new progress tracker spanning multiple courses.

The redesign led to a 19% increase in course completion rates and higher satisfaction scores in quarterly NPS surveys. education ux-microinteractions-2023 Microinteractions That Make Interfaces Feel Effortless microinteractions-that-delight A practical guide to designing subtle moments that guide and reassure users. Microinteractions are small design moments with an outsized impact on perceived usability.

In this article we break down four categories of microinteractions—input feedback, status and progress, navigational hints, and error recovery—showing real examples from recent client projects.

We also cover motion guidelines and accessibility considerations to ensure these moments feel helpful, not distracting. Ella Novak motion_design article ux-accessibility-2025 Leveling Up Accessibility in Product Teams accessibility-practices-for-product-teams Practical steps to embed accessibility into your design and development workflow. Accessibility is not a one-time checklist—it is an ongoing practice.

We share how we helped a SaaS team integrate accessibility into discovery, design, and QA. Topics include setting up shared color tokens, creating reusable accessible components, and running regular screen reader audits.

By the end of the project, critical accessibility issues dropped by 82% and the team felt confident shipping inclusive features. accessibility branding-restaurant-2024 From Pop-Up to Cult Favorite: Branding Ember Street Bistro branding-ember-street-bistro How a co

pages.body: Design Studio Graphic, Print, Web & Multimedia Agency Home Work Services Shop Blog Project Planner Favorites Cart (0) Project Planner Use this planner to outline complex design projects like multi-industry mobile apps. We will ask for a concise project name, a multi-sentence overview, launch timing, budget range, and your contact details. If you would like inspiration first, you can review more app case studies in our portfolio. Project name* Project overview* Aim for at least 2–3 sentences so we can understand context, constraints, and success metrics. Estimated budget range* Select a budget range Under $25,000 $25,000 – $50,000 $50,000 – $75,000 Over $75,000 Target launch date* An approximate date is fine. You can adjust later as the roadmap evolves. Contact details* Contact name* Contact email* We will use this email to follow up with next steps and clarifying questions. ← Submit project plan Back to portfolio case studies Design Studio A minimalist design agency portfolio and shop for thoughtful brands and digital products. pages.dataKeywords: japan Japan country Island nation in the Northwest Pacific; key region for temperate and subtropical marine mollusk diversity. australia Australia Continent-country in the Southern Hemisphere with extensive tropical to temperate marine habitats and diverse mollusk fauna. north_america North America continent Continent including Canada, the United States, Mexico, and Central America; includes both marine and freshwater mollusk faunas. western_atlantic Western Atlantic ocean_region Marine region comprising the western Atlantic Ocean from Newfoundland to southern South America, including the Caribbean Sea and Gulf of Mexico. indo_pacific Indo-Pacific Broad marine region spanning the tropical Indian and western Pacific Oceans; global hotspot for coral reef and mollusk diversity. cephalopod_camouflage_lesson_12_14_2021 Cephalopod Camouflage: Middle School Inquiry Lesson An inquiry-based lesson where students (ages 12–14) explore how octopuses, cuttlefish, and squid use dynamic skin patterns, chromatophores, and behavior to camouflage in different habitats. ages_12_14 Laura Kim Miguel Santos cephalopods camouflage behavior adaptation marine_biology cephalopod camouflage octopus squid cuttlefish chromatophores lesson plan middle school cephalopod_camouflage_virtual_lab_2020 Virtual Lab: Modeling Cephalopod Camouflage A browser-based virtual lab where students test how different background textures and lighting affect the visibility of a digital squid model that can change color and pattern. Nadia El-Masri simulation vision marine_ecology virtual lab computer activity octopus_arms_and_brains_ages_12_14_2019 Octopus Arms and Brains: Problem-Solving in Cephalopods A set of classroom activities introducing cephalopod neurobiology and problem-solving behavior using simple experiments and video analysis. Hanna Müller neurobiology problem solving inquiry activity intro_mollusks_8_11_2018 Introduction to Mollusks: Shells, Slugs, and Squid An introductory lesson for younger students covering the main mollusk groups with simple classification and observation activities. ages_8_11 Rachel Porter mollusks classification biodiversity snails bivalves elementary deep_sea_cephalopods_15_18_2022 Deep-Sea Cephalopods and Extreme Environments A high-school level lesson exploring adaptations of deep-sea squids and octopuses, including low-light vision, buoyancy, and bioluminescence. ages_15_18 Marco De Luca deep_sea bioluminescence deep sea high school bivalve_filter_feeding_12_14_2020 Filter-Feeding Bivalves: Ecosystem Engineers Students investigate how mussels and clams filter water and influence water quality using simple classroom experiments. Elena García ecosystem_services physiology filter feeding mussels clams water quality tidal_zone_snails_all_ages_2017 Tidal Zone Snails Field Guide and Activity Pack A printable field guide and activity pack for exploring rocky shore snails and their adaptations to changing tides. all_ages Patrick O'Neill gastropods intertidal int

pages.dataKeywords: all_services All Services Browse all landscaping, lawn care, cleanup, installation, and design services offered by our team. lawn_care_maintenance Lawn Care & Maintenance Ongoing lawn mowing, trimming, and routine yard maintenance services for residential and commercial properties. seasonal_cleanups Seasonal Cleanups One-time spring and fall cleanups, leaf removal, and storm debris removal to get your yard ready for the season. installations Installations New lawn, plantings, patios, fire pits, and other landscape installations for front and back yards. design_build Landscape Design & Build Custom landscape design, including drought-tolerant plans, outdoor living spaces, and full yard makeovers. other Other Services Additional landscaping services and add-ons that don’t fit into the main categories. lawn_mowing Lawn Mowing Regular mowing of turf areas, including edging along walkways and driveways. leaf_removal Leaf Removal Collection and removal of fallen leaves from lawns, beds, and hardscape areas. hedge_trimming Hedge Trimming Shaping and trimming of hedges, shrubs, and bushes to maintain a neat appearance. flower_bed_mulching Flower Bed Mulching Installation or refresh of mulch in flower beds to improve appearance and retain moisture. spring_cleanup Spring Yard Cleanup One-time spring cleanup including debris pickup, light pruning, and first-of-season yard preparation. drought_tolerant_design Drought-Tolerant Design Landscape designs focused on low-water, drought-resistant plants and groundcovers. patio Patio Design and installation of stone or paver patios for outdoor living spaces. fire_pit Fire Pit Built-in or freestanding fire pit features integrated into patio or backyard designs. water_feature Water Feature Decorative ponds, fountains, or waterfalls to enhance outdoor spaces. outdoor_lighting Outdoor Lighting Low-voltage landscape lighting for pathways, patios, and architectural accents. general_landscaping General Landscaping Planting, bed creation, sod installation, and other general landscaping tasks. small_up_to_quarter_acre Up to 1/4 acre medium_quarter_to_half_acre Medium (1/4–1/2 acre) large_half_to_one_acre Large (1/2–1 acre) extra_large_over_one_acre Extra Large (over 1 acre) res_mow_leaf_basic_small residential Basic Mow & Leaf - Small Yard Entry-level residential maintenance for small yards, including lawn mowing and light leaf removal twice per month. monthly res_mow_leaf_plus_small Plus Mow & Leaf - Small Yard Our most popular small-yard plan with weekly visits for consistent mowing and leaf cleanup. quarterly res_mow_only_small Lawn Mowing Only - Small Yard Budget-friendly mowing-only plan for small residential lawns. res_leaf_only_small Leaf Cleanup Only - Small Yard Recurring leaf removal service for properties that already have mowing covered. res_mow_leaf_medium Mow & Leaf - Medium Yard Twice-monthly mowing and leaf removal for medium-size residential properties. res_premium_large_full_care Premium Full-Care - Large Y
