pages.dataKeywords: severe_toothache_after_hours severe_toothache Severe Toothache (Including After 8:00 PM) If you have a severe, throbbing toothache that does not improve with over‑the‑counter pain relievers, you may have an infection or abscess that requires urgent care. Do not ignore worsening pain, fever, facial swelling, or difficulty swallowing. Rinse gently with warm salt water, floss carefully around the painful tooth to remove trapped food, and take an over‑the‑counter pain reliever as directed if you are able. Do NOT place aspirin directly on the tooth or gums, and avoid very hot or cold foods and drinks. 555-0199 555-0100 For severe toothache occurring after 8:00 PM (for example, around 9:00 PM), call the after‑hours emergency number and leave a detailed voicemail with your name, phone number, symptoms, and when the pain began. If you develop trouble breathing or swallowing, call 911 or go to the nearest emergency room. broken_tooth_chipped broken_tooth Broken or Chipped Tooth A broken, cracked, or chipped tooth can be painful and may expose the inner layers of the tooth, increasing the risk of infection. Rinse your mouth gently with warm water. If there is bleeding, apply gentle pressure with clean gauze. Save any broken pieces in milk or saline if possible. Avoid chewing on the affected side. Call immediately if you have significant pain, a large portion of the tooth is missing, or there is sharp edge cutting your tongue or cheek. Minor chips without pain can typically be seen within a few days during normal business hours. knocked_out_tooth_avulsed knocked_out_tooth Knocked‑Out Permanent Tooth A knocked‑out permanent tooth is a true dental emergency. Prompt treatment (ideally within 30–60 minutes) gives the best chance to save the tooth. Handle the tooth only by the crown (chewing surface), not the root. If dirty, gently rinse with milk or saline without scrubbing. If possible, place the tooth back into the socket and hold it in place by biting gently on gauze. If you cannot reinsert it, keep the tooth moist in milk or a tooth preservation kit. Do not let the tooth dry out. Do not scrub or remove any attached tissue fragments. Seek emergency dental care immediately. For children, primary (baby) teeth that are knocked out are generally NOT reinserted—call for guidance. uncontrolled_bleeding bleeding Uncontrolled Oral Bleeding Bleeding after dental work or an injury should gradually slow down with firm pressure. If bleeding is heavy or does not decrease after 20–30 minutes, it requires urgent assessment. Place a clean, folded piece of gauze or a damp tea bag over the area and bite down with firm, continuous pressure for 20 minutes without checking. Sit upright and avoid spitting or using straws. If bleeding is profuse, you feel light‑headed, or you cannot control it with pressure, call the after‑hours number immediately. If you are on blood thinners or have a bleeding disorder and cannot reach us quickly, call 911 or go to the nearest emergency room. su

pages.dataKeywords: ages_5_8 Ages 5–8 Entry-level red and orange ball players developing basic coordination and love for the game. ages_9_12 Ages 9–12 Developing juniors focusing on fundamentals, rally skills, and basic match play. ages_13_17 Ages 13–17 Teen players building advanced technique, tactics, and competitive confidence. high_performance_13_18 High Performance Ages 13–18 Tournament-focused juniors training with high intensity and match-specific drills. serve_power_fundamentals 5 Fundamentals of Powerful Serve Technique fundamentals-of-powerful-serve-technique Learn the key elements of efficient serve technique so you can add effortless power without losing control. A powerful serve starts from the ground up. Focus on a consistent ball toss slightly in front of you, a full shoulder turn, and a relaxed wrist snap at contact. Use your legs to drive upward and into the court so your body weight flows toward your target. Practice a slow-motion service motion in front of a mirror to ingrain proper positions before adding speed. Finally, incorporate targeted serve drills like serving to quadrants to develop both power and accuracy in your serve technique. serve serve technique power fundamentals adult players second_serve_consistency Build a Reliable Second Serve Under Pressure reliable-second-serve-under-pressure Transform your second serve technique so you can swing with confidence instead of just pushing the ball in. second serve mental toughness tennis_injury_prevention_tips Top 10 Tennis Injury Prevention Tips tennis-injury-prevention-tips Reduce your risk of common tennis injuries with smart warm-ups, strength work, and recovery habits. fitness injury prevention warm-up strength recovery shoulder_health_for_tennis Shoulder Health for Tennis Players shoulder-health-for-tennis-players Protect your serving shoulder with simple mobility and strength exercises that promote long-term injury prevention. The repetitive overhead motion of serving places significant demands on the shoulder. To keep it healthy, incorporate external rotation exercises with a light band, scapular stability drills like wall slides, and thoracic spine mobility work. Limit your total number of serves in each practice and avoid dramatic changes in volume from week to week. If you feel sharp pain, stop and assess rather than playing through it—early rest is one of the most effective forms of injury prevention. shoulder mobility backhand_blueprint The Modern Topspin Backhand Blueprint modern-topspin-backhand-blueprint Upgrade your backhand with a simple, repeatable sequence from preparation to follow-through. A solid topspin backhand starts with early preparation and a strong shoulder turn. Use your non-dominant hand to help set the racquet and keep your chest sideways to the net as you load on your outside leg. As the ball approaches, drop the racquet head below the ball and swing up and out toward your target, finishing over your shoulder. Practice shadow swings, then progress to slow feeds

Loading mission… Mission Philosophy Coaches FAQs Bookings & cancellations Terms Privacy Contact Our philosophy Loading philosophy… Our coaching team A multidisciplinary team of coaches and practitioners supporting performance, recovery, and long-term wellbeing. Loading coaching team… FAQs Key answers about how DOCIA Sport consultations, classes, and programmes work. Loading FAQ summary… Looking for more detail? See the Resources area for in-depth articles, or contact us directly with specific questions. Bookings, sessions & cancellations DOCIA Sport offers 1:1 consultations, group classes, and multi-week programmes. Most sessions are booked in advance online; in some cases we can also arrange bespoke sessions for teams, clubs, or organisations on request. Consultations & assessments Consultation services (such as running technique reviews, strength coaching, nutrition support, and wellbeing coaching) are delivered in pre-defined time slots. Each ConsultationTimeSlot has a clearly indicated duration, price, and location (online, in-person, or hybrid). Payment is normally taken at checkout when you add a consultation to your cart. Full details of the coach, location, and what to prepare are shown before you confirm. Programmes & challenges Programmes (multi-week plans or courses) and short challenges are scheduled around clear start dates. Depending on the delivery format, you may work self-paced, as part of a cohort, or in a hybrid model with regular live touchpoints. Programme places are confirmed once your order has been completed. Access details are emailed ahead of your chosen start date. Cancellations & changes We understand that plans sometimes change. Our terms of service set out the full cancellation and reschedulin

About our outreach Our mission Our vision Core outreach values These values shape how we serve our neighbors across every outreach expression. Key outreach programs Explore a few of the primary ways we come alongside our community and find a place to serve or participate. Campuses Each campus is a local expression of the same outreach heart, rooted in its own neighborhood. Support our outreach work Your financial partnership helps sustain food pantries, neighborhood care, and practical support offered through each campus. Give to outreach Search Search outreach content Next steps After you read about our mission and campuses, you can use the program links on this page to explore serving and event opportunities that fit you. pages.dataKeywords: downtown_campus Downtown Campus 125 Peachtree St NE Atlanta 404-555-0100 Our central hub in the heart of downtown, hosting worship services, outreach programs, and weekday workshops including financial coaching classes. midtown_campus Midtown Campus 780 W Peachtree St NW 404-555-0102 A vibrant campus near transit and universities, focused on young adult ministry and community service projects. westside_campus Westside Campus 2100 Marietta Blvd NW 404-555-0103 Located in Atlanta’s Westside neighborhood, this campus hosts regular neighborhood cleanups and food distribution events. eastside_campus Eastside Campus 950 Glenwood Ave SE 404-555-0104 Serving the East Atlanta community with family-oriented ministries, youth outreach, and a weekly food pantry. cleveland_campus Cleveland Campus 1800 Superior Ave E Cleveland 216-555-0105 Our Cleveland campus supports neighborhood cleanups, outreach events, and partnerships with local food resources. grace_downtown_food_pantry Grace Downtown Food Pantry Church-based food pantry serving low-income residents near downtown Atlanta with a mix of shelf-stable items and fresh foods. food_pantry 130 Auburn Ave NE 404-555-0201 fresh_produce dry_goods canned_goods personal_care_items Open Wednesdays 3:00–6:00 PM and Saturdays 10:00 AM–12:00 PM. Fresh produce available most weeks. midtown_community_meals Midtown Community Meals Free hot dinner program serving anyone in need, located near Midtown Campus. meal_program 785 Peachtree St NE Fellowship Hall 404-555-0202 hot_meals kids_snacks Community dinner served Mondays, Tuesdays, and Thursdays 5:30–7:00 PM. westside_family_pantry Westside Family Pantry Neighborhood pantry focusing on families with children, offering groceries and basic household items. 2155 Marietta Blvd NW 404-555-0203 baby_supplies Open Tuesdays and Thursdays 10:00 AM–2:00 PM. Limited fresh produce available on Thursdays. eastside_fresh_market_pantry Eastside Fresh Market Pantry Choice-style pantry emphasizing fresh fruits and vegetables alongside shelf-stable items. 960 Glenwood Ave SE 404-555-0204 diabetes_friendly_options Open Wednesdays 4:00–7:00 PM and Fridays 1:00–4:00 PM. Fresh produce and some refrigerated items available. downtown_community_fridge Downtown Community Fridge Outdoor fridge and pantry with free, take-what-you-need food items, restocked daily by volunteers. community_fridge 45 Courtland St NE 404-555-0205 prepared_meals grab_and_go_snacks Outdoor fridge accessible daily 7:00 AM–9:00 PM. pages.dataKeywords:
faq_30_day_money_back_guarantee What is your 30-day money-back guarantee? 30-day-money-back-guarantee billing_refunds refund money-back guarantee 30-day guarantee billing cancellation faq_how_to_get_started How do I get started with the work-from-home program? You can get started in three simple steps:

1. Choose a starter plan on the Plans & Pricing page that matches your budget and support needs.
2. Complete the short application so we can understand your goals and recommended track.
3. Check your email for login details and follow the "Week 1" onboarding checklist.

Most members complete onboarding in under 60 minutes and book their first coaching session within the first week. how-to-get-started getting_started getting started onboarding new members faq_billing_how_refunds_work How do refunds and cancellations work? how-refunds-and-cancellations-work subscription faq_technical_requirements What are the technical requirements to join? You’ll need a reliable internet connection, a modern web browser (Chrome, Firefox, Safari, or Edge), and a device with audio and video capability for live sessions (laptop, desktop, or tablet).

Our learning portal is mobile-friendly for viewing lessons, but we recommend a laptop or desktop for implementation work and coaching calls. technical-requirements technical technical requirements system requirements devices internet faq_program_differences What’s the difference between plans and programs? Plans determine how you access our platform (level of support, coaching, and tools), while programs are the specific business tracks you follow, such as Email Marketing or Affiliate Sales.

You can start with any plan and then choose one or more programs inside your dashboard. Higher-tier plans typically include more one-on-one coaching and additional live group sessions for your chosen program. difference-between-plans-and-programs programs plans coaching support levels plan_quickstart_89 Quickstart Coaching Lite quickstart-coaching-lite Quickstart Lite Entry-level work-from-home starter plan with focused training and a dedicated kick-off coaching call. Mindset & Time Blocking Choosing Your First Offer Simple Online Funnel Setup Getting Your First 10 Leads Weekly Action Planning 1 x 30-minute 1-on-1 kickoff call 5 core implementation modules Email support for 30 days Access on desktop and mobile active plan_quickstart_129 Quickstart Coaching Plus quickstart-coaching-plus Quickstart Plus Starter plan with extra accountability and deeper training for launching your first work-from-home offer. Clarity & Goal Setting Offer Design Workshop Messaging & Simple Sales Pages Lead Generation Fundamentals Follow-Up & Nurture Sequences 90-Day Launch Roadmap 2 x 30-minute 1-on-1 coaching calls 6 step-by-step training modules Private community access for 60 days Downloadable scripts and templates plan_quickstart_149 Quickstart Coaching Pro quickstart-coaching-pro Quickstart Pro Our most complete starter plan under $150, combining 1-o

pages.dataKeywords: central_family_center Central Family Activity Center central 10 Civic Plaza, Central District A municipal family hub offering afternoon play sessions, parenting workshops, and summer activities for children aged 0–6. downtown_early_learning_hub Downtown Early Learning Hub downtown 220 Market Street, Downtown A combined nursery and activity hub with themed holiday programs, language workshops, and arts activities for toddlers and preschoolers. north_play_and_learn_center North Play & Learn Center north 85 Pine Ridge Road, North District A neighborhood nursery center that also hosts afternoon creative clubs, outdoor play sessions, and school‑holiday programs. east_riverside_activity_hall East Riverside Activity Hall east 34 Riverside Lane, East District A multi‑purpose municipal hall used for music & movement classes, summer camps, and weekend family events. south_garden_childcare_center South Garden Childcare & Activities Center south 412 Garden Avenue, South pages.dataKeywords: photo_comp_2022_01 First 90cm Round at Spring Valley Star locking onto the first oxer of our 90cm round at Spring Valley. I was nervous, but she took me to the fence like a pro. competitions Spring Valley Showgrounds photo_comp_2022_02 Tight Turn to the Double Second fence of the tricky two-stride double at Spring Valley. We practiced this turn for weeks at home. photo_comp_2022_03 Clearing the Water Tray Our first clear jump over a spooky water tray at the county show. Star hesitated for a stride, then flew. Greenfield County Show photo_comp_2022_04 First Red Ribbon Finish Walking out of the arena after our first red ribbon in the 80cm class. I could not stop smiling. photo_comp_2022_05 Evening Jump-Off Round Our fastest round of the season under the evening lights. Star felt light and focused over every fence. Riverside Equestrian Centre photo_comp_2022_06 Waiting at the In-Gate One last pat and a deep breath while we wait for the bell. Star watches the last rider leave the arena. photo_comp_2022_07 Final Fence of the Season The last fence of our 2022 competition season. We finished on a clear round and a huge boost of confidence. Oakridge Arena photo_comp_2022_08 Prizegiving Lap of Honor Star cantering the lap of honor with her ribbon flapping on the bridle. She clearly enjoyed the applause. photo_comp_2023_01 First 1m Class Warm-Up Stretching over a warm-up oxer before our first 1m class. A big milestone for both of us. Brightside Showpark photo_comp_2023_02 Checking the Course Map Studying the jumpoff course with Star half-dozing beside me. The calm before the adrenaline. photo_training_2024_01 Gridwork in the Outdoor Arena A line of placing poles and small crosses to help Star find her rhythm and improve her technique over fences. training Home Arena photo_training_2024_02 Canter Poles for Suppleness Using raised canter poles on a circle to encourage Star to sit and balance in the corners. photo_tricks_2025_01 First Attempt at Bow Star learning to stretch one leg forward and lower her head for the bow trick, rewarded with lots of scratches. Lower Paddock photo_portrait_2021_01 Golden Hour Portrait Star standing quietly in the field at sunset, ears pricked to the sound of dinner arriving. portraits Home Field photo_stable_2020_01 Morning Feed Time Star waiting patiently with her head over the stable door as I mix her morning feed. stable_life Main Barn photo_stable_2021_02 Post-Ride Cool Down Walking Star out after a schooling session, letting her stretch and relax before heading back to the stable. Farm Track tricks_next Which trick should Star learn next? Help me choose Star’s next trick to work on in our clicker training sessions. Options include Bow, Spanish walk, Lie down, and Kiss. newsletter_topics What kind of updates do you want more of on the blog? Select the topics you check the blog for most often so I can plan future posts and newsletter content. competition_goals_2026 What should our main competition goal be for the 2026 season

pages.dataKeywords: art_aging_1820_affinage_basics Affinage Basics: How European Cheesemakers Aged Cheese in the Early 1800s affinage-basics-early-1800s-europe An overview of simple but effective cheese aging practices used by European farmstead producers between 1800 and 1850. In the early 19th century, most European cheese aging, or affinage, took place in cellars, caves, and cool outbuildings. Temperature and humidity were controlled not with machines, but with thick stone walls, earth insulation, and careful seasonal timing... aging_maturation aging europe 19th_century cellars art_aging_1850_affineurs Rise of the Affineur: Professional Cheese Aging 1850–1900 rise-of-the-affineur-1850-1900 How specialist affineurs emerged in France, Switzerland, and Italy in the later 19th century. By the mid-19th century, urban demand for cheese had grown so rapidly that dedicated cheese agers—affineurs—became central to the supply chain. They purchased young wheels from rural producers and aged them in city warehouses and tunnel cellars... affineurs france trade art_aging_1900_cold_storage From Cellar to Cold Room: Cheese Aging 1900–1920 from-cellar-to-cold-room-1900-1920 Early experiments with mechanical refrigeration began reshaping cheese aging in the early 20th century. Around 1900, mechanical refrigeration entered dairies and warehouses in North America and Europe. Cheesemakers started to combine traditional cave aging with chilled rooms that allowed more consistent temperatures, especially in summer months... refrigeration technology 20th_century art_aging_1920_science Scientific Affinage: Cheese Maturation Between the Wars scientific-affinage-1920-1940 How microbiology and chemistry began to guide cheese maturation between 1920 and 1940. By the 1920s, university laboratories and dairy schools were analyzing the microbial life of cheese rinds and interiors. Cheesemakers increasingly used selected starter cultures and surface inoculations instead of relying solely on ambient flora... microbiology laboratory ripening 1920s art_aging_1940_wartime Wartime Rations and Aging: Cheese Maturation 1940–1950 wartime-rations-cheese-aging-1940-1950 Rationing and industrialization forced major changes in cheese aging practices during and after World War II. During World War II, many traditional aging caves were repurposed or fell into disuse, while governments prioritized fast-ripening, high-yield cheeses for ration programs. After 1945, aging techniques had to adapt to new milk supplies, technologies, and consumer tastes... world_war_ii rationing industry art_production_ancient_near_east Curds in Clay Pots: The Earliest Evidence of Cheesemaking curds-in-clay-pots-earliest-cheesemaking Archaeological traces of cheese production in the ancient Near East and southeastern Europe. Residues found on perforated ceramic vessels suggest that people in the Near East were separating curds and whey as early as 5500 BCE... production_techniques archaeology ancient near_east art_cultural_monasterie

pages.body: Magical Revue Booking Home Kids Parties Corporate Events Browse Shows All Performers Build Your Own Show Gift Cards Favorites My Bookings Cart Overview How it works Events we support Trust & safety About Magical Revue Booking Magical Revue Booking is a dedicated booking service for live magic. We connect you with a curated network of professional magicians and show packages so you can add a polished, theatrical experience to your event without the guesswork. Instead of scrolling endlessly through generic listings, you tell us what you are planning and we translate that into clear options: performers who fit your audience, recommended show formats, and realistic pricing. You stay focused on your guests while we handle the details that make a live show run smoothly. Every performer in our network is an active professional, used to working in homes, schools, and corporate venues. Our role is to be the bridge between you and the magician: answering questions, setting expectations, and making sure the show that arrives on the day matches what you discussed in advance. How it works Step 1 Share the basics of your event Tell us the date you are aiming for, your approximate guest count, and the type of event you are planning (kids birthday, corporate celebration, school assembly, or something else). This gives us enough context to start matching you with appropriate magicians and show formats. Step 2 Explore recommended magicians and shows Based on your event type, we surface shows and performers that regularly work in similar settings. You can review brief descriptions, typical running times, and starting prices so you understand what a realistic booking looks like before you commit to anything. Step 3 Fine-tune details with our team When you are close to a decision, we confirm practical details: venue access, sound needs, timing within your schedule, and any special requests. We coordinate directly with the performer so everyone has a shared plan and you are not relaying

pages.dataKeywords: addon_balloon_twisting_30 Balloon Twisting (30 minutes) Colorful balloon animals and hats for kids right after the main magic show. Ideal for birthday parties and school events. kids_birthday school_show generic_event addon_magic_workshop_30 Magic Workshop (30 minutes) Hands-on magic lesson where kids learn and keep 2–3 simple tricks. Best for ages 7–12 and small groups. corporate_event addon_extra_15_minutes_show Extra 15 Minutes of Magic Extend your main magic show with an additional 15 minutes of tricks and audience participation. adult_birthday addon_face_painting_60 Face Painting (60 minutes) Classic face painting for kids with safe, hypoallergenic paints. Works best for parties up to 20 children. addon_sound_system_upgrade Sound System & Microphone Upgrade Professional PA system and wireless microphone setup for larger rooms or outdoor events. base_kids_standard_45 Standard 45-minute magic show A high-energy, family-friendly 45-minute magic show with comedy, audience participation, and a special spotlight trick for the guest of honor. ages_7_10 base_kids_ages5_7_35_no_animals Ages 5–7 Interactive Magic (No Animals) A 30–35 minute interactive magic show tailored for ages 5–7 with visual effects and no live animals, perfect for schools and younger birthdays. ages_5_7 base_corporate_closeup_60 1-hour Corporate Close-Up Magic Strolling close-up magic for small groups, ideal for receptions, mixers, and cocktail hours at corporate events. adults base_corporate_stage_120 2-hour Corporate Stage + Strolling Package A 45-minute stage show plus 75 minutes of strolling magic for networking and social time at your corporate event. base_adult_birthday_parlor_60 1-hour Adult Birthday Parlor Show An intimate parlor-style magic show with mind-reading, comedy, and interactive routines for adult birthday parties. base_generic_all_ages_30 30-minute All-Ages Magic Sampler A flexible 30-minute all-ages magic show suitable for community events, family gatherings, and mixed-age audiences. all_ages gift_50 $50 email print_at_home gift_100 $100 postal_mail gift_150 $150 gift_200 $200 perf_elias_reed_sf Elias Reed - Close-Up Magic SF elias-reed-close-up-magic-sf Elias specializes in intimate, close-up magic with cards and everyday objects. Perfect for adult birthdays and upscale cocktail events around San Francisco. San Francisco sound_system microphone table music_player close_up_strolling Pricing starts at $180 for a 45-minute close-up set within San Francisco. perf_bay_city_illusions Bay City Illusions bay-city-illusions A full-service magic experience offering parlor, stage, and strolling performances for milestones and corporate functions across the Bay Area. backdrop lighting stage_show Base package pricing for small private events starts at $250. perf_seattle_stage_strolling Seattle Stage & Strolling Magic seattle-stage-and-strolling-magic Dynamic corporate magic for conferences, offsites, and holiday parties with a mix of stage illusions and roaming close-up

How we use your information 4. Data storage and security 5. Data retention 6. When we share your information 7. Sharing with performers 8. Cookies and similar technologies 9. Email and marketing communication 10. Your privacy rights and choices 11. International data transfers 12. Childrens privacy 13. Changes to this policy 14. How to contact us Privacy quick links Request a copy of your data Request deletion of your data Request a correction Update email preferences You can also email us directly at privacy@magicalrevuebooking.example. Please include your booking reference or account email so we can find the right records. Print this policy Open print-friendly view 1. Overview Magical Revue Booking is an online service that helps people discover, compare, and book magic shows and related entertainment for events such as kids birthdays, corporate events, school shows, and more. This privacy policy explains how we handle personal information when you browse our site, create custom packages, request quotes, contact performers, purchase gift cards, or place bookings. It also explains your choices and rights, and how to contact us with privacy requests. When we say personal information, we mean any information that identifies or can reasonably be linked to you, such as your name, contact details, booking history, or event details. 2. Information we collect We collect information in three main ways: information you provide directly, information we receive from your use of the site, and information related to

pages.dataKeywords: art_tour_diary_2024_eu_leg1 Tour Diary 2024: First Nights in Europe tour-diary-2024-first-nights-in-europe Kicking off the 2024 world tour in Berlin, late-night train rides, and the first time playing the new single live. Tour Diary Europe Tour Live Lena Park published art_tour_diary_2024_eu_leg2 Tour Diary 2024: Night Drives and Neon Streets tour-diary-2024-night-drives-and-neon-streets From Prague to Vienna, we chase sunrise on the highway and discover a new pre-show ritual. On the Road Noah Reyes art_tour_diary_2024_summer_festivals Tour Diary 2024: Summer Festivals and Sudden Storms tour-diary-2024-summer-festivals-and-sudden-storms Mud, lightning delays, and the thrill of hearing thousands sing your chorus back at a sunset festival slot. Summer festivals are chaos in the best way. One minute you’re backstage sharing a golf cart with a band you’ve idolized for years, the next you’re watching crews scramble to cover amps as storm clouds roll in. At one festival, a lightning delay pushed our set back by an hour, but the crowd stayed. When the sky finally cleared, the field turned into a choir. We cut the backing tracks for the last chorus of “City Lights” and let the crowd carry it. It was imperfect and absolutely unforgettable. Festivals Summer 2024 Mia Chen art_tour_diary_2024_fall_na Tour Diary 2024: Late-Night Diners Across North America tour-diary-2024-late-night-diners-north-america From Toronto to Los Angeles, we map the continent through coffee refills, jukeboxes, and corner booths. Fall on the road means hoodies, foggy windows, and the constant hunt for the best diner in every city. In Toronto we found a spot that served pancakes until 3 a.m., and the server slipped us a handwritten playlist of local bands. In Chicago, a cook recognized us from a festival livestream and requested “Aurora Dreams” while flipping burgers. By the time we reached Los Angeles, we had a full zine’s worth of polaroids, receipts, and scribbled lyrics born at sticky tables under neon lights. North America Food art_tour_diary_2023_world Tour Diary 2023: First Steps Onto Global Stages tour-diary-2023-first-global-stages Our first time playing outside our home country, learning to say thank you in five new languages. Nothing prepares you for the feeling of stepping onto a stage where no one shares your native language, but everyone knows your songs. In Tokyo, the crowd was shy at first, then exploded during the bridge of “Silver Lines.” In São Paulo, it was the opposite—loud from the first note. We learned how to order coffee, say thank you, and introduce ourselves in each country we visited. Every night, we left the stage a slightly different band than the night before. World Tour art_tour_diary_2022_first_run Tour Diary 2022: Our First Van, Our First Tour tour-diary-2022-first-van-first-tour Before buses and festivals, it was five friends, one van, and a map full of tiny clubs. The 2022 run was all duct tape, borrowed gear, and sleeping on floors. We broke down o

pages.dataKeywords: all_cruises all-cruises All Cruises & Tours Browse all available river cruises, sightseeing tours, dinner cruises, family trips, and private charters. sightseeing sightseeing-cruises Sightseeing Cruises Daytime river sightseeing cruises with live commentary or audio guides, ideal for first-time visitors. dinner_cruises dinner-cruises Dinner Cruises Evening and dinner cruises featuring onboard dining, often with live music and panoramic city views. sunset_evening sunset-and-evening-cruises Sunset & Evening Cruises Relaxed sunset and evening cruises, with options for welcome drinks and light snacks. family_friendly family-cruises Family-Friendly Cruises Family-oriented cruises with flexible seating, child pricing, and options that include lunch. private_charters private-charters Private Charters Customizable private boat charters for groups, including evening events and special occasions. old_town_pier Old Town Pier Historic pier located at the edge of Old Town, primary departure point for classic sightseeing and sunset cruises. 1 Riverside Walk, Old Town Riverview city_harbor_marina City Harbor Marina city_harbor Modern marina close to downtown hotels, used for most dinner cruises and private evening charters. 250 Harbor Lane, Downtown riverfront_dock_east Riverfront Dock East riverfront_east Convenient dock on the east riverfront, popular for family-friendly lunch cruises. 88 East Riverfront Ave marina_bay_pier Marina Bay Pier marina_bay Scenic pier near Marina Bay Park, departure point for longer scenic and nature-focused tours. 15 Marina Bay Park Road any_cruise_custom Any Cruise Gift Card Flexible gift card valid toward any cruise or tour, with a customizable amount. dinner_cruise_gift_100 Dinner Cruise Gift Card Give the gift of an evening on the water, ideal for two guests on a standard dinner cruise. specific_category sightseeing_voucher_fixed Sightseeing Cruise Voucher Prepaid voucher for daytime sightseeing cruises, perfect for visitors and tourists. private_charter_credit Private Charter Credit Higher-value gift card intended as credit toward private charters and group events. sunset_city_lights_cruise Sunset City Lights Cruise 90-minute shared sunset cruise from Old Town Pier with a welcome drink and skyline views. Drift past the city’s famous riverfront landmarks as the sun sets and the skyline lights up. This relaxed 90-minute cruise departs from Old Town Pier and includes a welcome drink, open-air and indoor seating, and an audio guide with stories about the river’s history. shared_cruise sunset evening drinks_included welcome_drink sunset_theme evening_theme audio_guide free_cancellation Free cancellation up to 24 hours before departure; full charge applies within 24 hours. per_person 19:30 monday tuesday wednesday thursday friday saturday sunday Check in at the Old Town Pier ticket booth at least 20 minutes before departure. Light jacket, camera, and comfortable shoes. twilight_cocktail_cruise Twilight Skyline Cocktail Cruise 2-hou

pages.dataKeywords: devops DevOps Articles on CI/CD, infrastructure as code, automation, and DevOps culture. kubernetes Kubernetes Guides and case studies about running workloads on Kubernetes. monitoring Monitoring & Observability Posts on monitoring, logging, tracing, and observability practices. AWS Content focused on building and operating systems on Amazon Web Services. terraform Terraform Infrastructure as Code patterns and modules built with Terraform. microservices Microservices Architecture and implementation details for microservices-based systems. career Career Thoughts on engineering careers, hiring, and personal growth. role_senior_devops_acme Senior DevOps Engineer Acme Cloud Systems Berlin, Germany Leading the DevOps function for a multi-region SaaS platform with a strong focus on Kubernetes, CI/CD, and observability. Designed and maintained GitOps-based delivery pipelines for over 40 microservices on Kubernetes. Owned the observability stack, standardizing metrics, logs, and traces across all services. Led migration of legacy Jenkins pipelines to GitHub Actions, improving deployment frequency and reliability. Collaborated with security to implement policy-as-code and continuous compliance checks. Argo CD GitHub Actions Helm Prometheus Grafana OpenTelemetry role_devops_startupx DevOps Engineer StartupX Labs Remote First DevOps hire, responsible for building CI/CD pipelines and managing the Kubernetes-based platform. Implemented CI/CD pipelines that reduced average lead time to production from days to under one hour. Managed multi-tenant Kubernetes clusters used by development, staging, and production environments. Defined backup, disaster recovery, and incident response procedures. Automated infrastructure provisioning using Terraform and AWS modules. Jenkins GitLab CI Docker Elastic Stack Ansible role_sre_cloudscale Site Reliability Engineer CloudScale Analytics Munich, Germany SRE for a real-time analytics platform processing billions of events per day. Defined SLOs and error budgets in partnership with product teams. Built alerting and on-call runbooks to reduce mean time to recovery. Optimized Kubernetes resource usage, cutting infrastructure spend by 20%. Introduced chaos engineering experiments to validate resilience. Istio Python role_platform_datahub Platform Engineer DataHub Solutions Hamburg, Germany platform_engineer Built and maintained internal platform services used by multiple product teams. Developed self-service tooling for provisioning application environments. Maintained shared CI/CD templates and Docker base images. Collaborated with developers on performance and scalability improvements. Node.js PostgreSQL role_devops_oldbank OldBank AG Frankfurt, Germany Introduced DevOps practices within a regulated financial environment. Automated build and deployment processes for core banking services. Implemented configuration management and standardized environment provisioning. Worked with security and compliance teams to align pipelines w

pages.dataKeywords: art_under_10k_2026_overview How to Plan a Beautiful Wedding Under $10,000 (2026 Edition) plan-wedding-under-10000-2026 budget_money A step-by-step guide to planning a stylish wedding on a budget of $10,000 or less, including sample allocations and saving tips. Planning a wedding under $10,000 is absolutely possible with clear priorities and a realistic budget. In this guide, we break down venue, food, attire, decor, photography and more, showing sample allocations for different guest counts. You'll also find cost-saving strategies like off-peak dates, brunch receptions, and borrowing decor. Under $10,000 50–120 guests budget under_10000 planning_basics sample_budget art_under_10k_brunch_cityhall City Hall Ceremony & Brunch Reception: $10,000 All-In Sample Budget city-hall-brunch-10000-sample-budget See exactly how to allocate a $10,000 budget for a city hall ceremony followed by a brunch reception for 60–80 guests. This article walks through a sample $10,000 budget for a city hall ceremony and brunch reception. We cover permit fees, photography coverage, a brunch buffet, limited bar, simple florals, and a small dessert table. A detailed line-item budget shows how each dollar is allocated. 60–80 guests city_hall brunch art_under_10k_backyard_80_guests Backyard Wedding Budget: Under $10,000 for 80 Guests backyard-wedding-budget-under-10000-80-guests A realistic budget breakdown for hosting 70–80 guests in a backyard wedding for less than $10,000. Backyard weddings can save on venue fees but add costs for rentals, restrooms, and lighting. This article lays out a sample budget under $10,000, including tenting, basic rentals, buffet catering, and DIY decor. We also discuss where you can safely cut costs and where you shouldn’t. 70–80 guests backyard art_under_10k_micro_wedding Micro Wedding Budget Under $10,000: Intimate Celebration micro-wedding-budget-under-10000 Budget outline for a 20–30 person micro wedding with a $10,000 cap, focusing on great food and photography. For couples who value experience over a large guest list, a micro wedding can stretch a $10,000 budget incredibly far. This guide shows a sample budget focusing on a high-quality meal, stunning photography, and meaningful details, plus tips for trimming the guest list thoughtfully. 20–30 guests micro_wedding art_12k_15k_for_150 Stretching a $12,000–$15,000 Wedding Budget for 150 Guests stretch-12000-15000-budget-150-guests How to host around 150 guests on a $12k–$15k budget with venue, catering, and decor strategies. Hosting 150 guests on a mid-range budget requires trade-offs. We break down a sample $13,500 budget, highlight cost-per-guest targets, and share specific negotiation tips for venues and caterers. $12,000–$15,000 Around 150 guests (130–170) 150_guests art_save_vs_splurge Where to Save and Where to Splurge on Your Wedding Budget where-to-save-where-to-splurge-wedding-budget A practical guide to deciding which wedding elements deserve a splurge and where you can cut costs. N

pages.dataKeywords: youth_scholarship_fund Youth Scholarship Fund Provides need-based tuition assistance so that no young singer is turned away due to financial hardship. general_operations_fund General Operations Fund Supports the choir’s greatest needs, including music purchases, staff, and program development. touring_ensemble_fund Touring Ensemble Fund Helps cover travel, lodging, and outreach costs for our touring choirs. under_20000 Under $20,000 20000_39999 $20,000–$39,999 40000_59999 $40,000–$59,999 60000_89999 $60,000–$89,999 90000_plus $90,000 and above main_campus Main Campus 1500 Harmony Ave Riverton Primary rehearsal and audition location with multiple choir rooms and a small performance hall. downtown Downtown Center 225 Market Street Suite 300 Easily accessible by public transit; hosts community workshops and adult programs. north_side North Side Arts Annex 4800 Oak Park Blvd Satellite location used for select rehearsals and outreach concerts. eastside_church Eastside Community Church 910 Sunrise Way Partner venue for holiday concerts and large combined-choir rehearsals. daily Daily Updates Receive an email whenever there is news, usually a few times per week. weekly Weekly Roundup A summary of upcoming concerts, auditions, and news every week. monthly Monthly Newsletter Once-a-month email with highlights, stories, and key dates. monthly_digest Monthly Digest Condensed version of our monthly newsletter with just the essentials. upcoming_concerts Upcoming Concerts Performance announcements, ticket offers, and program details. scholarships_financial_aid Scholarships & Financial Aid Deadlines, application tips, and updates about tuition assistance. volunteer_opportunities Volunteer Opportunities Ushering, front-of-house roles, and other ways to get involved. organization_news Organization News Season announcements, leadership updates, and impact stories. community_workshops Community Workshops Free and low-cost community singing and education programs. need_based_tuition Need-based Tuition Scholarship Provides tuition assistance based on demonstrated financial need for singers enrolled in any youth ensemble. merit_based Merit-based Scholarship Awards partial or full tuition based on audition results, dedication, and leadership. other_support other Other Support & Fee Waivers Covers other program fees such as tour participation or uniform costs on a case-by-case basis. 2023_2024 2023–2024 2024_2025 2024–2025 2025_2026 2025–2026 2026_2027 2026–2027 prelude_choir Prelude Choir An introductory ensemble for young singers focusing on pitch matching, basic rhythm, and the joy of group singing. monday 16:15 17:15 training_chorus Training Chorus Builds foundational music literacy, part-singing, and healthy vocal production for developing singers. wednesday 16:30 17:45 lyric_treble_choir Lyric Treble Choir For intermediate treble voices ready for more challenging repertoire and performances. tuesday Your contact details Email address * First name * Last name * Organization (optional) If you are signing up on behalf of a school, choir, or organization, you may note it here. pages.body: Choral Arts Society Youth Choir & Scholarship Home Choirs & Ensembles Join / Auditions Concerts & Tickets Community Programs Scholarships & Aid Volunteer Donate Newsletter Scholarships & Tuition Aid Review available tuition assistance options and submit a scholarship application for an upcoming choir season. Scholarship type Season Household income guidance Income information helps our scholarship committee distribute limited funds as equitably as possible. Please select the range that best reflects your household's annual income before taxes. Skip to scholarship application Dismiss Scholarship application Applying for: , Singer information Singer first name* Singer last name* Singer date of birth* Is the singer a current choir member?* Yes No Parent / guardian contact Parent or guardian name* Email address* Phone number (optional) Financial information Household annual income range* Brief description of financial need* Please share any information that will help the committee understand your family's situation. A few sentences are sufficient. Submit scholarship application Scholarship applications are not available We are not currently able to accept scholarship applications because application options are unavailable. Please check back later or contact our office for assistance. Choral Arts Society Nurturing young voices through choral music and education. pages.body: Yakkyofy Search the Help Center Help Center Home Shipping & Fulfillment Store setup Store Integrations Payments & Payouts Support & Messaging Billing & subscriptions System status Help Center / Contact Support / Support Request Form Support Request Fill in the details below to submit a ticket to the Yakkyofy support team. 1. Category & topic Tell us what your request is about so we can route it to the right team. Category Loading categories... For example: Billing & subscriptions, Shipping & fulfillment. Topic Select a category first Choose the option that best matches your question or request. 2. Account details Include the account and store information we should look at when reviewing your request. Loading service details… Engagement models & pricing Project-based overview Hourly UX research Most UX research and testing work is delivered as scoped projects. We define outcomes, participants, methods, and timelines up front, then quote a fixed or phased fee. Use the hourly tab if you prefer a flexible block of expert UX research time. Best for well-defined initiatives like redesign discovery, remote usability testing rounds, or benchmark studies. Includes planning, research ops, facilitation, analysis, and recommendations as appropriate. Use the contact page or project estimator if you are not sure how to scope your project yet. pages.dataKeywords: ts_basic_local Basic Talent Scouting – Local TS_BASIC_LOCAL Entry-level talent scouting plan for local searches and occasional bookings. talent_scouting basic Search and view up to 200 talent profiles per month Basic filters: location, category, gender, age Create and manage 2 favorites lists Shortlist up to 25 talents per list In-site messaging with up to 20 talents per month ts_basic_plus Basic Talent Scouting – Plus TS_BASIC_PLUS Enhanced basic plan for small agencies needing more outreach capacity. Search and view up to 500 talent profiles per month Advanced filters including rating and hourly rate Create and manage 5 favorites lists Shortlist up to 50 talents per list Unlimited in-site messaging Export shortlisted talent as CSV ts_standard Standard Talent Scouting TS_STANDARD For casting directors and brands running recurring campaigns. standard Unlimited profile views Full filter access including social reach and language Create and manage 20 favorites lists Collaborative shortlists with team comments Priority support Basic analytics on casting performance ts_premium Premium Talent Scouting TS_PREMIUM High-volume plan for agencies booking talent across multiple regions. premium Unlimited profile views and searches Dedicated account manager Custom saved search alerts API access for internal tools Team workspaces and role-based permissions Advanced analytics and reporting ts_basic_annual_only Basic Talent Scouting – Annual TS_BASIC_ANNUAL Discounted annual-only basic scouting plan for long-term users. Same features as Basic Talent Scouting – Local Additional 10% discount on event tickets Annual invoicing and consolidated receipts tp_basic Basic Talent Profile TP_BASIC Starter profile plan for new models and actors building their presence. talent_profile Public talent profile with up to 10 photos One primary category and location Receive direct booking requests Apply to up to 5 castings per month In-site messaging with clients tp_premium Premium Talent Profile TP_PREMIUM Visibility-boosting plan for established talent. Unlimited photos and 5 video reels Multiple categories and locations Profile spotlight in search results Advanced availability calendar Priority support for booking issues Profile performance insights ea_basic Basic Event Access EA_BASIC Occasional access to workshops and open calls. event_access Access to public workshops and seminars Member pricing on select events Register for up to 3 events per month Email reminders for upcoming events ea_standard Standard Event Access EA_STANDARD For talent and clients attending events regularly. Unlimited registrations for eligible events Priority waitlist for sold-out workshops Exclusive member-only sessions Downloadable event materials and recordings corp_enterprise Enterprise Agency Suite CORP_ENTERPRISE Custom solution for large agencies combining scouting, profiles, and events. other enterprise All Premium Talent Scouting features Unlimited Talent Profile upgrades for roster Bulk even

pages.dataKeywords: xr-museums-storytelling-2025 Reimagining Museum Storytelling with Immersive XR reimagining-museum-storytelling-with-immersive-xr How museums and galleries are using VR and AR to turn static exhibits into interactive, narrative-driven journeys. Museums around the world are embracing XR to extend their narratives beyond the glass case. From room-scale VR recreations of historical events to AR layers that reveal hidden details on artifacts, immersive technologies are transforming passive visits into participatory experiences. In this article we break down successful concepts, hardware considerations, visitor flow design, and accessibility best practices for curators planning their first XR pilot. Dana Morales museums_exhibitions museum exhibition curation storytelling ar-gallery-tours-2025 Designing AR Gallery Tours That Don’t Distract from the Art designing-ar-gallery-tours-that-dont-distract-from-the-art A practical guide for galleries using mobile AR and headsets to enhance, not overshadow, exhibitions. When introducing AR into gallery spaces, curators often worry that screens will pull attention away from the art itself. The key is to design a companion experience: light-touch overlays, context-aware prompts, and audio-first storytelling that supports the work on the wall. This guide explores optimal interaction patterns, device policies, and analytics for AR gallery tours, with examples from pilot programs across Europe and North America. Leah Anders gallery mobile visitor-experience immersive-exhibition-roadmap From Static Exhibit to Immersive Installation: A 6-Month Roadmap from-static-exhibit-to-immersive-installation-roadmap Timeline, roles, and budget checkpoints for turning a traditional exhibition into an immersive XR installation. Transitioning from traditional vitrines to immersive XR spaces doesn’t have to be overwhelming. In this roadmap we outline a practical six-month plan: discovery and stakeholder alignment, concept prototyping, content production, technical integration, on-site testing, and launch. We highlight decision points unique to museums, like conservation concerns, visitor safety, and staffing models. project-planning budgeting museums xr-for-open-air-festivals XR for Open-Air Festivals and Temporary Exhibitions xr-for-open-air-festivals-and-temporary-exhibitions Tech, logistics, and creative concepts for XR activations at festivals and pop-up exhibitions. Temporary exhibitions and festivals introduce unique XR challenges: weather, connectivity, variable light, and throughput. This article outlines deployment patterns for AR scavenger hunts, projection-mapped stages, and pop-up VR domes, with a focus on quick setup and teardown, rugged hardware, and staff training. Marcus Li festival pop-up logistics retail-vr-try-before-you-buy VR ‘Try Before You Buy’: What Actually Works in Retail vr-try-before-you-buy-what-works-in-retail Lessons from VR try-on and product visualization pilots across fashion, furniture, and automotive
